item.title: 在家工作的 15 项有用服务 | 15 Helpful Services For Working From Home
item.description:
许多员工享受在家工作带来的好处。 有些人觉得很幸运能花更多时间陪伴家人或避免通勤。 其他人喜欢灵活地设置自己的时间并围绕学校或日托的时间表工作。


近年来，越来越多的人转向远程工作，开发人员发布了越来越多的新服务和应用程序，有助于让远程工作更加舒适。 我为您准备了一份新的、不同的、有用的远程工作服务列表，这将进一步简化团队合作并提高您的个人效率！

团队合作——有些人认为远程工作是带薪休假，这会导致效率和绩效下降。 如果您的团队中有这样的员工，请为每个人安装 Teamwork 服务，借助它，您可以每小时跟踪一次，以提高团队的生产力。 该工具允许您对所有跟踪的时间和费用进行计费。 您可以创建有关在项目上花费的时间的自定义报告。 以 Excel、CSV 或 PDF 格式导出报告和时间日志记录。

混合工作不可或缺的工具——您可以随时在家中连接到办公室计算机。 也是技术支持人员和系统管理员必备的软件。 连接是通过链接，有快速支持和永久访问。

电子邮件分析- 一种工具，可让您通过使用电子邮件来提高团队内部的生产力和活动。 用户可以查看电子邮件的平均响应时间，以及每个团队成员每天接收和发送的电子邮件数量。 该工具对远程销售人员和营销人员特别有用。

允许管理员添加员工并控制对工作区的访问。 该服务可以与 Dropbox 和 Google Drive 等重要应用程序集成。 信使不仅可以用于通信，还可以用于交换文件、音频和视频通话。

队友可以互相发送证书、徽章、奖励和视频，并附上感谢和善意的信息。 员工还可以选择通过礼品卡、赃物或自定义奖励发送礼物。 该程序的运作方式类似于社交信息，让远方的同事保持联系和参与。

它消除了通话双方的背景噪音，因此您和您的来电者可以从任何地方接听重要电话。 您可以将它与任何通信应用程序（Zoom、Skype、Slack 和许多其他应用程序）一起使用，并享受更高效的远程会议。

一个方便的工具，用于跟踪有关您的主题的最新新闻和出版物。 无需在数十个书签、订阅和社交媒体渠道之间纠结，您可以将它们全部收集在一个地方，并将它们作为一个单一的提要阅读。 它对内容经理、记者和 SMM 经理特别有用。

在搜索框中，您可以输入感兴趣的主题和竞争对手的名称或特定资源的 URL，以获取相关出版物及其有效性/病毒性的详细概述。 搜索结果可以通过几个参数过滤，只留下您想要的信息。

它允许您创建项目、任务、事件、帖子和消息模板，以及计划出版物、广告和媒体活动。 该服务还提供有关您的营销活动的详细分析。

直观的在线视频编辑器，包含大量剪辑和信息图表。 制作一个小型宣传视频很容易。 但是，在免费版本中，您将不得不忍受水印。

该软件报告其他远程工作应用程序的使用情况，并跟踪团队成员之间的互动，以便管理人员更好地了解员工如何在线度过工作时间。

该程序在一个中央存储库中收集信息，远程团队成员可以快速访问和搜索以找到他们需要的信息。 该平台对于在家独立工作的员工来说是一个很好的自助服务工具。 员工可以轻松找到问题的答案，而无需给同事发短信和等待回复，并在工作时保持社区意识。

该程序创建了一个通用邮箱，以便员工可以将客户的问题和请求直接发送到一个中央枢纽。 远程团队可以更好地管理工作流程并批准或分配任务，而不必担心其他团队成员已经在处理该问题。

该在线平台集成了工资单、福利、员工信息和合规工具。 同时，Justworks 简化了雇主的工资单、医疗保健登记和记录保存。

它支持多种格式，包括 PSD、PXD、JPEG、PNG。 有一个非常有用的功能可以从照片中删除背景，还有大量的滤镜、设计模板和动画可供选择。



原文:

On May 17 in the USA celebrates the holiday of all freelancers — National Work From Home Day! Many employees enjoy the benefits that come with working from home. Some people feel lucky to spend more time with their family or avoid a commute. Other people like the flexibility to set their own hours and work around schedules for school or daycare.


In recent years, more and more people are moving to remote work, and developers are releasing more and more new services and applications that help make remote work even more comfortable. I have prepared for you a list of new, different, and useful services for remote work, which will further simplify teamwork and increase your personal efficiency!

Teamwork — some people perceive remote work as a paid vacation, which leads to a decrease in efficiency and performance. If you have such employees in your team, install Teamwork service for everyone, with its help you can track every hour to increase the productivity of your team. The tool allows you to bill for all tracked time and expenses. You can create custom reports on time spent on a project. Export reports and time log records in Excel, CSV or PDF format.

Getscreen.me — a cloud service for remote access via a browser. An indispensable tool for hybrid work — you can connect from home to your office computer at any time. Also a must-have software for technical support staff and system administrators. Connection is by link, there is Quick Support and Permanent Access.

Email Analytics — a tool that allows you to increase productivity and activity within your team by using email. Users can see the average response time to emails, as well as the number of emails each team member receives and sends daily. This tool will be especially useful for remote salespeople and marketers.

Troop Messenger — a worthy alternative to the popular Slack, suitable for working in small teams. Allows administrators to add employees and regulate access to the workspace. The service can be integrated with important applications such as Dropbox and Google Drive. The messenger can be used not only to correspond but also to exchange files, audio and video calls.

Kudos — is an employee recognition program that facilitates peer-to-peer shoutouts between remote colleagues. Teammates can send each other certificates, badges, awards, and videos accompanied by thanks and kind messages. Employees can also choose to send presents from gift cards, swag, or custom rewards. The program operates like a social feed, keeping distant colleagues connected and engaged.

Krisp — is a noise-canceling application that helps remote workers and teams work more productively. It eliminates background noise from both sides of the call, so you and your callers can take important calls from anywhere. You can use it with any communication app (Zoom, Skype, Slack and many others) and enjoy more productive remote meetings.

Feedly — smart newsfeed. A handy tool for keeping track of the latest news and publications on your topic. Instead of being torn between dozens of bookmarks, subscriptions and social media channels, you can gather them all in one place and read them as a single feed. It will be especially useful for content managers, journalists and SMM managers.

BuzzSumo — content analytics service, allows you to identify trends, current topics and viral content, as well as determine opinion leaders in different topics. In the search box, you can enter both the topic of interest and the name of a competitor or URL of a specific resource to get a detailed overview of related publications and their effectiveness/virality. Search results can be filtered by several parameters to leave only the information you want.

CoSchedule — A free tool for organizing marketing activities. It allows you to create projects, tasks, events, post and message templates, as well as plan publications, advertising and media campaigns. Detailed analytics on your marketing activities are also available in the service.

Bitable — service for creating branded videos. Intuitive online video editor with a large library of clips and infographics. It’s easy to put together a small promotional video. However, in the free version, you will have to put up with a watermark.

ActiveTrac — is remote workforce management software that tracks the performance of remote teams. The software reports on the use of other remote work applications and tracks interactions between team members so that managers can better understand how employees are spending their work time online.

Bloomfire — is a collaborative knowledge management platform. The program collects information in one central repository that remote team members can quickly access and search to find what they need. The platform is a great self-service tool for employees working independently at home. Employees can easily find answers to questions without having to text colleagues and wait for a response, and maintain a sense of community as they work.

Front — is a customer communication tool that integrates all forms of messaging into a single platform. The program creates a universal mailbox so that employees can direct customer questions and requests to one central hub. Remote teams can better manage workflows and approve or assign tasks without worrying that another team member is already working on the issue.

Justworks — is a professional employer organization, or PEO, a human resources service provider serving small and medium-sized businesses. The online platform integrates payroll, benefits, employee information and compliance tools. Justworks offers a centralized online portal to which remote workers can apply for time off, enroll in benefits plans or obtain company documents such as handbooks or organizational charts. At the same time, Justworks simplifies payroll, health care enrollment, and record-keeping for employers.

Pixlr — advanced online photo processing tool. It supports many formats, including PSD, PXD, JPEG, PNG. There is a very useful function to remove backgrounds from photos, as well as a large selection of filters, design templates and animations.
item.author: getscreen

item.title: useEffect 与 useLayoutEffect | useEffect vs useLayoutEffect
item.description:
乍一看，它似乎用于相同的目的，但用例与它的核心原理存在差异。

使用效果

当钩子稳定时，如果您重构任何类组件以使用钩子，您可能会将任何代码从componentDidMount 、 componentDidUpdate和componentWillUnmount移动到useEffect 。

一个问题是，它在 react 渲染您的组件后运行，并确保您的效果回调不会阻止浏览器绘制。 这与在渲染后同步运行componentDidMount和componentDidUpdate的类组件中的行为不同。 这种方式性能更高，大多数时候这就是你想要的。

但是，如果您的效果正在改变 DOM（通过 DOM 节点 ref）并且 DOM 突变将在渲染和您的效果改变它之间改变 DOM 节点的外观，那么您不想使用useEffect . 你会想要使用useLayoutEffect 。 否则，当您的 DOM 突变生效时，用户可能会看到闪烁。 这几乎是您想要避免useEffect并改用useLayoutEffect的唯一时间。

使用布局效果

这会在 React 执行完所有 DOM 突变后立即同步运行。 如果您需要进行 DOM 测量（例如获取元素的滚动位置或其他样式），然后进行 DOM 突变或通过更新状态触发同步重新渲染，这将很有用。

至于调度，这与componentDidMount和componentDidUpdate的工作方式相同。 您的代码在 DOM 更新后立即运行，但在浏览器有机会“绘制”这些更改之前（用户实际上直到浏览器重新绘制后才看到更新）。

概括

  • 一种特殊情况

    您可能想要使用useLayoutEffect而不是useEffect的另一种情况是，如果您正在更新一个值（如 ref），并且您希望在任何其他代码运行之前确保它是最新的。

    例如：

     const ref = React.useRef() React.useEffect(() => { ref.current = 'some value' }) // then, later in another hook or something React.useLayoutEffect(() => { console.log(ref.current) // <-- this logs an old value because this runs first! })

    所以在这种情况下，解决方案是useLayoutEffect 。

    结论

    这都是关于默认值的。 默认行为是让浏览器在 React 运行代码之前根据 DOM 更新重新绘制。 这意味着您的代码不会阻塞浏览器，并且用户可以更快地看到 DOM 的更新。 所以大部分时间都坚持使用useEffect 。



原文:

At the first glance, it seems like to be used for same purpose, but there are differentials of use cases from it's core principles.

useEffect

useEffect is the one we use 99% in the React function component. When hooks are stable and if you refactor any of your class components to use hooks, you'll likely move any code from componentDidMount, componentDidUpdate, and componentWillUnmountto useEffect.

The one catch is that this runs after react renders your component and ensures that your effect callback does not block browser painting. This differs from the behavior in class components where componentDidMount and componentDidUpdate run synchronously after rendering. It's more performant this way and most of the time this is what you want.

However, if your effect is mutating the DOM (via a DOM node ref) and the DOM mutation will change the appearance of the DOM node between the time that it is rendered and your effect mutates it, then you don't want to use useEffect. You'll want to use useLayoutEffect. Otherwise the user could see a flicker when your DOM mutations take effect. This is pretty much the only time you want to avoid useEffect and use useLayoutEffect instead.

useLayoutEffect

This runs synchronously immediately after React has performed all DOM mutations. This can be useful if you need to make DOM measurements (like getting the scroll position or other styles for an element) and then make DOM mutations or trigger a synchronous re-render by updating state.

As far as scheduling, this works the same way as componentDidMount and componentDidUpdate. Your code runs immediately after the DOM has been updated, but before the browser has had a chance to "paint" those changes (the user doesn't actually see the updates until after the browser has repainted).

Summary

  • useLayoutEffect: If you need to mutate the DOM and/or do need to perform measurements
  • useEffect: If you don't need to interact with the DOM at all or your DOM changes are unobservable (seriously, most of the time you should use this).

One special case

One other situation you might want to use useLayoutEffect instead of useEffect is if you're updating a value (like a ref) and you want to make sure it's up-to-date before any other code runs.

For example:

const ref = React.useRef()
React.useEffect(() => {
  ref.current = 'some value'
})

// then, later in another hook or something
React.useLayoutEffect(() => {
  console.log(ref.current) // <-- this logs an old value because this runs first!
})

So in situations like that, the solution is useLayoutEffect.

Conclusion

It's all about defaults. The default behavior is to let the browser re-paint based on DOM updates before React runs your code. This means your code won't block the browser and the user sees updates to the DOM sooner. So stick with useEffect most of the time.
item.author: satel

item.title: 提升营销策略的 5 个 IP 定位技巧 | 5 IP Targeting Tips To Boost Your Marketing Strategy
item.description:
适合消费者需求的营销已经存在了很长一段时间。 我们都有研究产品的经验，然后在几个小时后在我们的社交媒体或其他地方得到建议。

这显然是将人们与他们真正想要的产品联系起来的好方法。 研究表明，当品牌提供个性化体验时， 80% 的消费者更有可能进行购买。

但是，公司如何才能建立一种营销策略，既能触及正确的目标群体，又不会浪费金钱为错误的人做广告呢？

以下是使用 IP 定位来提升营销策略的五个技巧。

什么是 IP 定位？

首先，什么是IP？ 因此，IP 定位是一种通过 IP 地址定位您的用户并将您的广告投放到他们的特定网络和设备的策略。

详细了解您的客户

  • 提供定制的用户体验
  • 准确的人口统计用户定位
  • 获得有关流量、销售额等指标的见解。
  • 产生意识
  • 节省资源

    • 跟踪 IP 最直接的方法是使用IP API 。 这些工具为您提供有关国家和城市的客户位置信息以及时区、纬度和经度等其他相关信息。

    通过地理位置或网络。 假设您的定位策略基于地理位置或地理定位。 在这种情况下，您可以通过他们的 WiFi 或 GPS 数据处理有关其居住国家、州、城市和社区的数据。
    如果您将 IP 定位基于网络，您还将获得有关互联网提供商、公司和域名的信息。

    创建 IP 定位策略的 5 个技巧

    如果您希望改善您的潜在客户生成或用户体验，这五个技巧将在此过程中为您提供帮助。

    为了找到您的 Nemo，正确研究它们至关重要。 虽然一些解决方案提供了广泛的功能，但其他解决方案保持简单并提供基本功能。 适合您的 IP API 将取决于您的需求和预算。

    仅当您的目标设置正确时，IP 定位才会起作用。 否则，您将无法获得预期的结果。

    首先创建一个简单的买家角色，包括性别、年龄和兴趣。 尽可能多地收集有关您的人口统计的有价值数据，以缩小您的 IP 定位列表。

    从这里开始，不要在整个地方传播您的营销活动，而是将其缩小到特定的地理位置。 考虑您想要的客户类型，并将您的见解与您网站的回报分析交叉引用。 例如，在您的客户居住或经常学生居住的地区，他们将登录大学的 WiFi。 这将是提供学生折扣的好地方。 您的人口统计使用的设备类型也有助于成功的活动。

    您对客户的需求和偏好了解得越多，您的活动就越个性化。

    准备好目标群体后，您必须确保您的广告系列脱颖而出。 如果通用设计不能吸引您的客户，那么单独定位不会让您走得太远。

    幸运的是，IP 跟踪也可以帮助您解决这个问题。 毕竟，它允许您个性化您的内容。 例如，了解用户的位置后，您可以以他们的母语提供内容或以他们的货币显示价格。 无论您决定采用哪种方法，请确保您与客户建立联系。

    如果您遵循提示一和二，但仍然难以看到结果，则问题可能出在其他地方。

    人们越来越倾向于使用 VPN 或广告拦截器。 虽然它们为个人提供安全性，但 VPN 对公司来说可能是一种痛苦，因为它们可以支持恶意流量。 但是，有一些工具可以帮助您解决这个问题。

    但是，也许问题有所不同。 找出最有效的方法的唯一真正方法是对其进行测试。 通过对您的人口统计或标准进行较小的更改进行测试，并将两个或多个广告系列与另一个广告系列进行比较。 虽然这最初会更昂贵，但从长远来看，它会更有利可图。

    虽然 IP 跟踪的最大好处是能够将您的目标受众指定到（几乎）最小的细节，但这也可能成为一个挑战。

    如果您选择的目标群体过于狭窄，例如只有 18 至 20 岁的心理学学生，您将失去潜在客户。 如果您过于具体，则会遗漏部分受众，从而限制您的 IP 定位成功。

    虽然 IP 跟踪听起来像是很多工作，但回报可能是巨大的。 通过个性化的活动改善您的客户体验可能会让您在竞争中处于领先地位。

    通过根据他们的需求创建定制的活动来与您的受众建立联系，并记住设计与正确的人口统计同样重要。 测试您的策略，从您的见解中学习并改进您的业务。



    原文:

    Marketing that fits the consumer's needs has been around for quite a while now. We all had experiences researching a product and then getting it suggested hours later on our social media or somewhere else.

    This obviously is a great way to connect people with the products they really want. Studies show that 80% of consumers are more likely to make a purchase when brands offer a personalized experience.

    But how can companies build a marketing strategy that reaches the right target group without wasting money on ads for the wrong people?

    Here are five tips on boosting your marketing strategy by using IP targeting.

    What Is IP Targeting?

    First of all, what even is an IP? IP stands for Internet Protocol. An IP is a unique numerical code that each internet-enabled device, like phones, computers, and tablets, has.
    Therefore, IP targeting is a strategy that targets your users through their IP addresses and delivers your ads to their specific networks and devices.

    IP Targeting is widely used for and can help you with:

    • Learning more about your customers
    • Offering a customized user experience
    • Accurate demographic user targeting
    • Gaining insights on metrics like traffic, sales, etc.
    • Generating awareness
    • Saving resources

    The most straightforward way to track IPs is by using an IP API. These tools offer you information on your customers' location regarding countries and cities and other relevant information like time zones, latitudes, and longitudes.

    How Does IP Targeting Work?

    There are two forms of IP targeting; either by geolocation or by network. Suppose you base your targeting strategy on geolocation or geotargeting. In that case, you can work with data about their country of residence, state, city, and neighborhood via their WiFi or GPS data.
    If you base your IP targeting on the network, you will also get information about the internet provider, the company, and the domain name.

    5 Tips on creating an IP targeting strategy

    If you are looking to improve your lead generation or user experience, these five tips will help you along the way.

    1. Research IP APIs
    IP APIs are like fish in the sea, but not all of them are Nemo. In order to find your Nemo, it is vital to research them properly. While some solutions offer a wide range of functionality, others keep it simple and down to the essential features. The suitable IP API for you will depend on your needs and budget.

    2. Select the right demographic
    IP targeting will only work if your target is set correctly. Otherwise, you won't get the hoped-for results.

    Start by creating a simple buyer persona that includes gender, age, and interests. Collect as much valuable data as possible on your demographic to narrow down your IP targeting list.

    From here on, instead of broadcasting your marketing campaign all over the place, narrow it down to specific geographical locations. Think about the type of customers you want and cross-reference your insights with your website's return analytics. In the areas your customers live in or frequent students, for example, will log into the university's WiFi. This would be a great place to offer a student discount. The type of device your demographic uses can also contribute to a successful campaign.

    The more you know about your customers' needs and preferences, the more personalized your campaign will be.

    3. Stand out

    Once you have your target group ready, you have to ensure that your campaign stands out. Targeting alone won't get you far if a generic design doesn't engage your customers.

    Luckily IP tracking can also help you out with this. After all, it allows you to personalize your content. Knowing your users' location, for example, allows you to provide content in their native language or show your prices in their currency. Whatever approach you decide on, make sure you connect with your customers.

    4. Testing, Testing, Testing

    If you followed tips one and two but still struggle with seeing results, the problem might lie elsewhere.

    People are more and more inclined to use VPNs or ad blockers. VPNs allow users to show a location different from their actual one. While they provide security for individuals, VPNs can be a pain for companies as they can support malicious traffic. There are, however, tools that help you move around this problem.

    However, maybe the issue is something different. The only real way to find out what works best is by testing it out. Test by making smaller changes in your demographic or criteria and compare two or more campaigns with another. While this will be more pricey initially, it will be more profitable in the long run.

    5. Don't be too specific
    While the great benefit of IP tracking is being able to specify your target audience to the (almost) smallest detail, this can also become a challenge.

    If you choose a target group that is too narrow, for example, only 18 to 20-year-old psychology students, you will lose out on potential customers. If you are too specific, you leave out parts of your audience, limiting your IP targeting success.

    IP tracking: Personalized and customized

    While IP tracking sounds like a lot of work, the payoff can be huge. Improving your customer experience through personalized campaigns might set you ahead of your competition.

    Connect with your audience by creating a customized campaign to their needs, and remember that the design is as important as the right demographic. Test out your strategies, learn from your insights and improve your business.
item.author: elli

item.title: 你应该知道的高级 Django 查询 | Advanced Django Queries you should know
item.description:
乌斯曼·阿巴斯

发表于在处理大型应用程序时，尤其是那些涉及高数据库计算的应用程序，例如具有银行或支付功能的金融技术产品，编写优化和高效查询的需求对于业务的成功至关重要。 本文涵盖了作为 Django 开发人员应该知道的一些常见的高级查询。

先决条件

为了理解这篇文章，您应该对以下内容有一些基本到中级的知识（尽管如果您只是对数据库查询的工作原理感到好奇，您仍然可以阅读）：

  • 面向对象编程
  • 对象关系映射 (ORM) 和 Django 模型
  • 查询集

为了演示起见，将使用以下模型：

使用的模型是足球运动员、团队和比赛。 一名足球运动员属于一个球队，而一个球队有许多足球运动员（ManytoOne 关系）。 此外，一个足球运动员可以参加许多比赛，一个比赛可以有许多足球运动员参加（ManyToMany 关系）。 注意：这种关系可以在团队和比赛之间起作用。 这只是为了演示。

为方便起见，数据库已预先填充了虚拟数据，如下所示：

现在我们已经解决了这个问题，让我们继续学习其中的一些查询。

下面的查询获取在一个赛季中至少打进 20 球或更多球的所有足球运动员。

上述查询中的数字“20”是一个常数。 但是，如果您想根据模型（或为简单起见的表）中的另一个字段进行搜索，该怎么办？ 例如，您希望获得所有助攻数超过本赛季得分的球员。 这就是“F”表达式的用武之地。 F() 的一个实例用作对模型中字段的引用。 一个例子如下所示：

通过使用“F”表达式，您可以将模型中的一个字段与另一个字段进行比较。

用于复杂查询的“Q”

下面的查询获取所有年龄在 30 岁以上且在一个赛季中进球数超过 20 的足球运动员：

默认情况下，filter() 方法中的关键字参数被评估为“AND”。 所以上面的查询相当于写了下面的 SQL 查询：“SELECT * FROM Fotballer WHERE age > 30 AND season_goals > 20”。

但是如果你想用 OR 而不是 AND 来执行复杂的查询呢？ 假设您想要获得所有年龄超过 30 或在一个赛季中进球超过 20 的球员。 请参阅下面的查询：

请注意 Mohammed Salah 是如何添加到查询集结果中的，因为他低于 30 岁。这是一个简单的示例，但不限于您可以使用“Q”做什么。 您还可以使用 & 代替 | 来执行复杂和查询上面使用的那个。 重要的是要注意，您可以在“Q”旁边使用 ~ 来执行否定。 请参阅下面的查询：

上面的查询选择了所有不超过 30 岁但进球或助攻达到两位数的球员。 这是 SQL 等价物：
注解

有时您希望对查询的结果执行额外的计算或计算。 虽然这可以在使用 python 获得查询结果后完成，但出于性能原因，最好允许数据库处理计算。 例如，如果想要获得每个足球运动员的进球贡献（这将是进球和助攻的总和），这将是一个很好的注释用例。 请参阅下面的查询：

如上所示，目标和助攻在数据库中汇总并作为指定的字段名称（goal_contributions）返回。 这比获取所有足球运动员然后必须循环通过查询集来执行求和更优化。

聚合

注释对查询集中的每个对象执行计算，而聚合对整个查询集执行计算。 例如，如果您想获得所有足球运动员的所有进球总数，请使用以下查询：

请注意，查询的结果是一个字典，键是方法的参数。 查询中的“Sum”称为聚合函数，其中有几个。 您还应该观察到注释因此是查询集中每个对象的聚合。

选择相关（）

如果使用得当，这可能是一个令人难以置信的性能提升器。 考虑以下两个查询：

如图所示，第一个查询命中数据库两次，而第二个查询只命中数据库一次。 在处理非常大的数据集时，这种看似很小的差异可能会极大地提高性能。

与 select_related() 方法类似，prefetch_related() 也用于减轻连接多个表所产生的开销。 这意味着 selected_related() 方法只能用于外键和 OneToOne 关系。
下面的代码演示了 prefetch_related() 的使用：

然而，关于 prefetch_related() 需要注意的一点是，对结果查询集执行诸如过滤器之类的子查询会导致再次查询数据库并破坏 prefetch_related() 的全部目的。 阅读更多内容：

感谢阅读！！！

资源：

https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.2/ref/models/querysets/ https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.2/ref/models/querysets/
https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.2/topics/db/queries/ https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.2/topics/db/queries/

Divyessh Maheshwari - Divyessh Maheshwari - May 16 5月16日

Iryna Hvozdetska - Iryna Hvozdetska - May 16 5月16日



原文:

Usman Abass

Posted on May 17 • Originally published at Medium

When working on large applications, especially those that involve high database computation like financial technology products with banking or payments generally, the need to write optimised and efficient queries becomes very crucial to the success of the business. This article covers some common advanced queries you should know as a Django developer.

PREREQUISITES

In order to understand this article, you should have some basic to mid-level knowledge of the following (although you can still read if you're just curious about how database queries work):

  • Python
  • Object Oriented Programming
  • Object Relational Mapping (ORM) and Django models
  • Querysets

For demonstration sake, the following models will be used:

The models used are Footballer, Team and Competition. A footballer belongs to one team, and a team has many footballers (ManytoOne relationship). Also, a footballer can participate in many competitions and a competition can have many footballers participating in it (ManyToMany relationship). Note: The relationship can work between Team and Competition. This is just for demonstration sake.

For convenience, the database has been prepopulated with dummy data as shown below:

Now that we've gotten that out of the way, let's move on to learning about some of these queries.

"F" EXPRESSIONS

The query below gets all footballers who have scored at least 20 goals or more in a season.

The number "20" in the above query is a constant. However, what if you want to base your search on another field in the model (or table for simplicity)? For instance, You want to get all players who have assisted more than they've scored in the season. That is where "F" expressions come in. An instance of F() serves as a reference to a field in the model. An example is shown below:

By using "F" expressions, you are able to compare a field in the model with another field.

"Q" FOR COMPLEX QUERIES

The query below gets all footballers that are above 30 and have scored over 20 goals in a season:

By default, keyword arguments in the filter() method are evaluated as "ANDs". So the above query is equivalent to writing the following SQL Query: " SELECT * FROM Fotballer WHERE age > 30 AND season_goals > 20 ".

But what if you want to perform complex queries with OR instead of AND? Suppose you want to get all players that are above 30 OR have scored over 20 goals in a season. See the query below:

Notice how Mohammed Salah has been added to the queryset result because he is below 30. This is a simple example of, but not limited to what you can use "Q" for. You can also perform complex and queries by using & instead of | that was used above. It is important to note that you can use ~ alongside "Q" to perform negation. See the query below:

The query above selects all players that are NOT up to 30, but hit double figures in either goals scored or assists given. This is the SQL equivalent of:
" SELECT * FROM Footballer WHERE NOT age > 30 AND (season_goals >= 10 OR season_assists > 10) ".

ANNOTATION

Sometimes you want to perform additional calculations or computations on results from a query. Although this can be done after obtaining the results of the query using python, it's best to allow the database to handle the computations for performance reasons. For instance, if want to get the goal contributions (which would be a sum of both the goals scored and assists given) for each footballer, this would be an excellent use case for annotations. See the query below:

As shown above, the goals and assists are summed up in the database and returned as the field name specified (goal_contributions). This is more optimal than fetching all footballers and then having to loop through the queryset to perform the sum.

AGGREGATION

While annotations perform computations on each object in the queryset, aggregations perform computations on the ENTIRE queryset. The result of the aggregate() method is a dictionary with each argument in the method serving as the dictionary's key. For instance, if you want to get a total of all goals scored by all footballers, you use the query below:

Notice that the result of the query is a dictionary with the key being the method's argument. "Sum" in the query is called an aggregation function and there are several of those. You should also observe that annotations are therefore aggregations on EACH object in the queryset.

select_related()

This can be an incredible performance booster when used correctly. The select_related() method returns a queryset that fetches the related model by performing a JOIN on the related field. Consider the two queries below:

As shown, the first query hits the database twice while the second query hits the database just once. This seemingly little difference can be a huge performance booster when dealing with very large datasets.

prefetch_related()

Similar to the select_related() method, the prefetch_related() is also used to mitigate the overhead resulting from joining multiple tables. The prefetch_related() method does a separate lookup for each relationship and performs the JOIN in python. This means that the selected_related() method can only be used for Foreign Key and OneToOne relationships.
The code below demonstrates the use of prefetch_related():

One thing to note about prefetch_related() however, is that performing a subquery such as filter on the resulting queryset would lead to the database being queried again and defeating the entire purpose of prefetch_related(). Read more on this at:

https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.2/ref/models/querysets/#prefetch-related

Thank you for reading!!!

Resources:

https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.2/ref/models/querysets/
https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.2/topics/db/queries/

item.title: ¿ Es siempre necesario usar un framework de javascript en desarrollo web? | ¿Es siempre necesario usar un framework de javascript en desarrollo web?
item.description:
重新激活。 Escuchar los cambios de variables relevantes de nuestra aplicación y disparar cambios en la UI o en otras partes del código en consecuencia Escuchar los cambios de variables relatedes de nuestra aplicación y disparar cambios en la UI o en otras partes del código en consecuencia

Sin embargo, a día de hoy parece que no hay otra opción más que usar estos frameworks. Sin embargo, a díade hoy parece que no hay otra opción más que usar estos frameworks。 Como todo en programación, la virtud esta en saber elegir cuándo usar qué tecnología sin complicarse la vida en exceso. Como todo en programación, la virtud esta en sabre elegir cuándo usar qué tecnología sin complicarse la vida en exceso。

Estos frameworks están disponibles como parte del registro de npmjs y tienen muchas dependencias de terceros que a su vez tienen las propias. Estos frameworks están disponibles como parte del registro de npmjs y tienen muchas dependencias de terceros que a su vez tienen las propias。 Así mismo, el proceso por el cual generan los archivos de despliegue es sumamente complejo y la forma en la que funcionan está sumamente abstraída. Así mismo, el proceso por el cual generan los archivos de despliegue es sumamente complejo y la forma en la que funcionan está sumamente abstraída。

Con su llegada hemos aprendido a depender en exceso de las dependencias, optimizaciones y procesos que gestionan por nosotros , esto no es malo pero si puede ser inconveniente. Con su llegada hemos aprendido adepender en exceso de lasdependencias, optimizaciones y procesos que gestionan por nosotros , esto no es malo pero si puede ser inconveniente。

La tela de araña que tejen todas estas dependencias puede volverse en tu contra y perder todo el tiempo que ganas usándolos lidiando con los problemas que dan los diferentes gestores de paquetes y las dependencias transitivas de tu aplicación . La tela de araña que tejen todas estasdependencias puede volverse en tu contra y perder todo el tiempo que ganas usándolos lidiando con los problemas que dan los diferentes gestores de paquetes y las dependencias transivas de tu aplicación 。

También tendremos que lidiar con webpack , rollup o cualquier otro packager y alejarnos del javascript vanilla para aprender una forma completamente nueva y específica de hacer las cosas. También tenremos que Lidiar con webpack ， rollup o cualquier otro packager y alejarnos del javascript vanilla para aprender una forma completamente nueva y específica de hacer las cosas。 Por así decirlo, quedaremos atrapados en un entorno, en un nicho que en unos años puede que esté anticuado. Por así decirlo, quedaremos atrapados en un entorno, en un nicho que en unos años puede que esté anticuado。

El mensaje de este post no es: ' Jamás uses frameworks de SPA ', es más bien: ' Decide de forma correcta CUANDO debes usarlos '. El mensaje de este post no es: ' Jamás uses frameworks de SPA ', es más bien: ' Decide de forma correcta CUANDO debes usarlos '。 Analiza si los beneficios superan los inconvenientes. Analiza si los beneficios superan los inconvenientes。

Construir una aplicación web minúscula o un conjunto de páginas estáticas (si, podemos usar static rendering pero eso es algo que ya tienes si escribes directamente el código HTML, CSS y Javascript sin procesar 🙂) bajo este paraguas no tiene mucho sentido. Construir una aplicación web minúscula o un conjunto de páginas estáticas (si, podemos usar static rendering pero eso es algo que ya tienes si escribes directamente el código HTML, CSS y Javascript sin procesar 🙂) bajo este paraguas no tiene mucho sentido。

En cambio, si tu aplicación es exageradamente dinámica, con muchas actualizaciones, interacción continua del usuario y necesitas modularizarla debido a su tamaño y complejidad si deberías plantearte la elección de un framework . En cambio, si tu aplicación es exageradamente dinámica, con muchas actualizaciones, interacción continua del usuario y necesitas modulesizarla debido a su tamaño y complejidad si deberías plantearte la elección de un framework 。

Gracias por leerme, ¡Nos vemos en la próxima! Gracias por leerme, ¡Nos vemos en la proxima! Déjame un comentario con tu opinión 🦝. Déjame un comentario con tu opinión 🦝。



原文:

Allá por 2014, cuando Angular fue anunciado toda la comunidad dev se sumó al entusiasmo de tener una herramienta más bajo el cinturón a la hora de desarrollar aplicaciones web. Con el tiempo, otros frameworks de javascript como React, Vue y Svelte se sumaron a esta moda.

Los motivos principales, entre muchos otros, que justificaban el uso de estas tecnologías eran los siguientes:

  1. Facilitar el desarrollo web mediante una herramienta que precompilara la lógica, los estilos y aplicara optimizaciones (Typescript, SCSS, compresiones, placeholders...)
  2. Reutilizar componentes web. HTML tiene su propia especificación extendiendo de HTMLElement, puedes leer más en este enlace
  3. Simplificar la generación estática y el renderizado en el servidor (en el caso de React con frameworks como Next.js y en Svelte con SvelteKit, previamente Sapper)
  4. Reactividad. Escuchar los cambios de variables relevantes de nuestra aplicación y disparar cambios en la UI o en otras partes del código en consecuencia

Sin embargo, a día de hoy parece que no hay otra opción más que usar estos frameworks. Como todo en programación, la virtud esta en saber elegir cuándo usar qué tecnología sin complicarse la vida en exceso.

Estos frameworks están disponibles como parte del registro de npmjs y tienen muchas dependencias de terceros que a su vez tienen las propias. Así mismo, el proceso por el cual generan los archivos de despliegue es sumamente complejo y la forma en la que funcionan está sumamente abstraída.

Con su llegada hemos aprendido a depender en exceso de las dependencias, optimizaciones y procesos que gestionan por nosotros, esto no es malo pero si puede ser inconveniente.

La tela de araña que tejen todas estas dependencias puede volverse en tu contra y perder todo el tiempo que ganas usándolos lidiando con los problemas que dan los diferentes gestores de paquetes y las dependencias transitivas de tu aplicación.

También tendremos que lidiar con webpack, rollup o cualquier otro packager y alejarnos del javascript vanilla para aprender una forma completamente nueva y específica de hacer las cosas. Por así decirlo, quedaremos atrapados en un entorno, en un nicho que en unos años puede que esté anticuado.

El mensaje de este post no es: 'Jamás uses frameworks de SPA', es más bien: 'Decide de forma correcta CUANDO debes usarlos'. Analiza si los beneficios superan los inconvenientes.

Construir una aplicación web minúscula o un conjunto de páginas estáticas (si, podemos usar static rendering pero eso es algo que ya tienes si escribes directamente el código HTML, CSS y Javascript sin procesar 🙂) bajo este paraguas no tiene mucho sentido.

En cambio, si tu aplicación es exageradamente dinámica, con muchas actualizaciones, interacción continua del usuario y necesitas modularizarla debido a su tamaño y complejidad si deberías plantearte la elección de un framework.

Gracias por leerme, ¡Nos vemos en la próxima! Déjame un comentario con tu opinión 🦝.

item.title: 您所有每日新闻的唯一新闻机器人 | The Only News Bot for your all Daily News
item.description:
最好以技术迷的身份阅读新闻，以跟上技术世界的最新动态。

也很难跟上网站和新闻； 访问每个页面只是为了查看新内容需要时间。 跟踪科技新闻网站也很困难。

将所有这些新闻网站和文章放在一个地方不是更方便吗？

因此，我开发了一个DevBudBot 。

在此处查看实时 Bot DevBudBot

那么，这到底是什么，它有什么作用？

所有网站网址都在命令中，您只需在命令中单击您喜欢的站点名称即可获取新闻。

到目前为止支持的网站：

您可以在GitHub 上找到该项目



原文:

It's preferable to read the news as a tech fan to keep up with what's new in the world of technology.

Also difficult to keep up with websites and news; visiting each page only to see what's new takes time. It's also difficult to keep track of tech news websites.

Isn't it more convenient to have all of those news websites and articles in one place?

As a result of this, I developed a DevBudBot.

Check out live Bot here DevBudBot

So, what exactly is this and what does it do?

DevBudBot is a Telegram bot that collects data from a variety of websites and provides article titles with embedded urls.

All of the website urls are in the command, and all you have to do is click on your preferred site name in the command to obtain the news.

Supported websites till now :

You can find the project on GitHub
