item.title: 减小 /var/log/journal 的大小 | Reduce size of /var/log/journal
item.description:
阿布登努尔

发表于您可以在 /etc/systemd/journald.conf 中使用此参数控制此目录的大小：

SystemMaxUse=50M

您可以强制进行日志轮换：

$ sudo systemctl kill --kill-who=main --signal=SIGUSR2 systemd-journald.service
 注意：如果上述信令方法不这样做，您可能需要重新启动日志服务以强制日志轮换。 您可以像这样重新启动服务：

sudo systemctl restart systemd-journald.service

亚历山大·奥莫罗昆瓦 - 亚历山大·奥莫罗昆瓦 - Feb 22 2月22日



原文:

abdennour

Posted on May 17

$ journalctl --disk-usage Journals take up 3.8G on disk.

You can control the size of this directory using this parameter in your /etc/systemd/journald.conf:

SystemMaxUse=50M

You can force a log rotation:

$ sudo systemctl kill --kill-who=main --signal=SIGUSR2 systemd-journald.service
NOTE: You might need to restart the logging service to force a log rotation, if the above signaling method does not do it. You can restart the service like so:

sudo systemctl restart systemd-journald.service

Alexander Omorokunwa - Feb 23

Alexander Omorokunwa - Feb 22
item.author: abdennour

item.title: 静态类型的终极讽刺 | The ultimate irony of static typing
item.description:
本文旨在展示静态类型在其安全性方面的某些方面。

考虑数学表达式：

 f(x) = x + 1 where x > 10

这个表达式很简单，即使对于那些认为他们不需要数学的程序员来说也是如此。

现在，请注意该函数是为任何大于 10 的 x 定义的。 例如，想象一个最小值为 11 的量表。

没有必要想太多的条件。 它只是一个任意选择的数字，用于演示编写所需函数的某些方面。

我们可以用 Python 或您选择的任何其他语言来制作它。

很简单，不是吗？

此外，我们将演示将 Python 称为强类型语言的含义。 对于字符串“11”，Python 会为我们抛出异常。

 # for string input "11" f ( "11" ) >>> TypeError (...)

虽然 Python 并不认为添加带有数字的字符串是一个好主意，但有些语言却不这么认为。

将其与弱类型 Javascript 进行比较：

通常，这不是我们希望看到的结果。

因此，我们认为强类型语言比弱类型语言更安全。

你的问题是：强类型等于静态类型吗？

答案是否定的！

动态类型不需要我们提前声明类型，而静态类型需要我们这样做，或者至少，我们需要为分析器提供足够的上下文来为我们推断类型。

由于 Python 允许支持可选的静态类型，让我们继续类型提示。 注意变量 x 的更改名称以突出显示潜在问题。

 def f ( bigger_than_ten : int ) -> int : return bigger_than_ten + 1

是不是更好更安全？ 奇怪的是，静态类型在骗我们。 我们不希望任何人认为小于 11 的整数是正确的输入，对吧？ 但它有效！

从数学的角度来看，这个函数的推理应该是这样的：既然保证输入大于 10，我们可以假设输出总是大于 11。

不幸的是，我们的代码不能保证这一点。 那么微小的修正呢？

 def f ( bigger_than_ten : int ) -> int : if bigger_than_ten > 10 : return bigger_than_ten + 1 else : raise ValueError ()

太棒了，不是吗？

现在，当涉及到静态类型时，您应该会看到最大的讽刺。

静态类型的支持者确实声称动态类型是不安全的，因为它会导致运行时异常，如下所示：

 f ( "11" ) >>> TypeError (...)

同时，当静态类型无法捕捉到意外的输入时，他们会毫不犹豫地把同样的灾难带到他们的头上。

如果您到目前为止一直在关注，那么关于动态和静态类型安全性的讨论应该是徒劳的，因为这里的主要罪魁祸首是引发可能会或可能不会被捕获的运行时异常的能力。

它们是不良副作用，应该避免。 在 Rust 或 Go 等最近的语言中，他们的设计者通过完全避免引发异常来解决这个问题。

感谢您的阅读。



原文:

This article aims to demonstrate certain aspects of static typing regarding its safety.

Consider the mathematical expression:

f(x) = x + 1 where x > 10

The expression is simple enough, even for coders who think they do not need mathematics.

Now, note the function is defined for any x bigger than 10. Imagine, for instance, a gauge with the lowest value being eleven.

There is no need to think too hard about the condition. It is just an arbitrarily chosen number to demonstrate certain aspects of writing the required function.

We can make it in Python or any other language of your choice.

Simple enough, isn't it?

Furthermore, we will demonstrate what it means when we call Python a strong-typed language. For string "11", Python will raise an exception for us.

# for string input "11"
 f("11") >>> TypeError(...)

While Python does not think adding strings with numbers is a great idea, some languages believe otherwise.

Compare it to weak-typed Javascript:

Usually, this is not the result we want to see.

Thus, we consider strong-typed languages safer than weak-typed ones.

The question for you is: Is strong-typed equal to statically-typed?

The answer is no!

Dynamic typing does not require us to declare types in advance, while static typing needs us to do so, or at least, we need to provide enough context for the analyzer to infer the types for us.

Since Python allows support for the optional static typing, let us proceed with type hints. Note the changed name of the variable x to highlight a potential problem.


def f(bigger_than_ten:int)->int: return bigger_than_ten + 1

Is it better and safer? Curiously enough, static typing is lying to us. We don't want anyone to think that an integer lower than 11 is the correct input, right? But it works!

From a mathematical perspective, the reasoning about the function should be as follows: since the input is guaranteed to be bigger than 10, we can assume the output is always bigger than 11.

Unfortunately, our code does not guarantee that. So what about a tiny correction?


def f(bigger_than_ten:int)->int: if bigger_than_ten > 10: return bigger_than_ten + 1 else: raise ValueError()

Wonderful, isn't it?

Right now, you should see the greatest irony when it comes to static typing.

Proponents of static typing do claim that dynamic typing is unsafe because it leads to exceptions in runtime, like this:

 f("11") >>> TypeError(...)

And in the same time, they do not hesitate to bring the very same disaster upon their heads when static typing fails to catch an unexpected input.

If you have followed so far, it should be evident that the discussion about safety in dynamic and static typing is futile since the main culprit here was the ability to raise runtime exceptions that might or might not be caught.

They are undesirable side effects and should be avoided. In recent languages like Rust or Go, their designers addressed the concern by avoiding raising exceptions at all.

Languages like Kotlin or F# tried to minimize the harmful impacts while staying compatible with the flagship languages of their platforms, i.e., Java and C#.

Thank you for reading.
item.author: hanpari

item.title: 哪个开源软件让您的日常职业生活变得轻松？ | Which open source software makes your daily professional life easy?
item.description:

  3. 如果您像我一样必须使用 SSH 管理多个服务器，或者可能通过 SSH 隧道访问多个服务，那么每次都打开您的终端然后查找 IP 和凭据是很糟糕的。 相反，我们可以使用 (termius) [ https://termius.com/ ] 这让生活变得如此轻松。 尽管它不是开源的，但它确实有一个适用于许多用例的免费计划。

item.title: 最终了解递归函数 | Finally Understanding Recursive Functions
item.description:
我曾经很难将注意力集中在递归函数上。 今天，我学习了递归的 3 个特性，使它们易于理解。

递归函数的3个特点：

  1. 递归函数具有由参数值表示的状态。
  2. 调用内部函数时，确保必须以某种方式修改参数（状态），使其更接近“中断条件”并最终满足它。
  3. 设置不再调用内部函数的“中断条件” （即中断递归）。

让我们看看例子。

功能#1

这将一直运行到堆栈溢出，因为它只是在不断地运行自己。

功能#2

 def run ( n ): # the state is represented by the value n run ( n ) # no break condition; no change of state (n) run ( 10 )

这也将一直运行，直到堆栈溢出，因为没有中断条件。

功能#3

 def run ( n ): if n == 10 : # break condition (always unsatisfied) run ( n ) # no change of state (n) run ( 10 )

这也将一直运行到堆栈溢出，因为状态 (n) 始终满足运行条件。

功能#4

 def run ( n ): if n == 10 : # break condition (always unsatisfied) run ( n ) # no change of state run ( 5 )

这将永远不会运行第二次，因为状态 (n) 永远不会满足再次运行的条件。

功能#5

 def run ( n ): if n < 1 : # breaking condition (satisfied until the state meets breaking condition) run ( n - 1 ) # continuous change of state run ( 10 )

这将递归运行，直到满足中断条件。

你有它。 递归函数具有由参数值表示的状态，并且需要最终满足函数的“中断条件”的状态 (n) 变化。

我希望这有帮助。



原文:

I used to have a hard time wrapping my mind around recursive function. Today, I learned 3 characteristics about recursion that makes them easy to understand.

The 3 characteristics of recursive functions:

  1. Recursive functions have a state that is represented by a parameter value.
  2. When calling the inner function, ensure that the parameter (state) must be modified in some way that brings it closer to the 'break condition' and eventually meet it.
  3. Set the 'break condition' for which the inner function is not called again (i.e. breaking the recursion).

Let's see examples.

Function #1

This will run until the stack overflows because it's just running itself continuously.

Function #2 

def run(n): # the state is represented by the value n
 run(n) # no break condition; no change of state (n)

run(10)

This will also run until the stack overflows because there is no breaking condition.

Function #3 

def run(n): if n == 10: # break condition (always unsatisfied)
 run(n) # no change of state (n)

run(10)

This will also run until the stack overflows because the state (n) always satisfies the condition for running.

Function #4 

def run(n): if n == 10: # break condition (always unsatisfied)
 run(n) # no change of state

run(5)

This will never run a second time because the state (n) never meets the conditions for running again.

Function #5 

def run(n): if n < 1: # breaking condition (satisfied until the state meets breaking condition)
 run(n-1) # continuous change of state

run(10)

This will run recursively until the breaking condition is met.

There you have it. A recursive function has a state that is represented by a parameter value and requires a change of state (n) which eventually meets the 'breaking condition' of the function.

I hope this helps.

item.title: MCIPFE - Cálculo Lambda | MCIPFE - Cálculo Lambda
item.description:
计算λ

乌玛索玛：

吨你是的一种是的是的一种吨你吨你吨你一种吨你一种一种吨你一种一种一种吨你吨你真 = \lambda x.\lambda y。 结论

书目

原文:

O Manual Completamente Incompleto de Programação Funcional com Elixir é uma série de postagens bem pequenas para explicar um pouco de programação funcional.

Cálculo lambda

"No início eram apenas funções"

O cálculo lambda é a abstração de um sistema formal da matemática lógica baseado na substituição e mapeamento de valores, sendo a base do conceito de funções puras que temos hoje.

Usando símbolo λ (lambda), seguido do nome do parâmetro e por fim a expressão.

Uma soma:

λx.  x+1(λx.  x+1)22+1=3 \lambda x.\;x + 1 \newline (\lambda x.\;x + 1)2 \newline 2+1 =3 λx.x+1(λx.x+1)22+1=3 

add_one = fn(x) -> x+1 end
add_one(2)
#3

No caso acima temos uma função simples que recebe um valor como parâmetro (x) e retorna a soma do parâmetro mais (+) 1, ou seja, substitui (x) por um valor e mapeia para expressão de (+1).

O poder de uma função

Com o conceito simples de mapeamento e substituição é possível construir qualquer coisa inclusive a lógica booleana.

Lógica booleana de forma extremamente resumida é ideia que no universo só existem dois estados Verdadeiro ou Falso, contendo operadores como E, OU e NÃO.

Vamos criar a lógica de Verdadeiro , Falso e o operador Negação.
O conceito de Negação é basicamente a inversão do valor booleano, dessa forma NÃO Falso é igual à Verdadeiro.

true=λx.λy.  xfalse=λx.λy.  y(λb.  b  false  true)true(true  false  true)=false(λb.  b  false  true)false(false  false  true)=true true = \lambda x.\lambda y. \;x \newline false = \lambda x.\lambda y. \;y \newline (\lambda b. \;b \;false \;true) true \newline (true\;false\;true) = false \newline (\lambda b.\; b \;false\; true) false \newline (false\;false\;true) = true true=λx.λy.xfalse=λx.λy.y(λb.bfalsetrue)true(truefalsetrue)=false(λb.bfalsetrue)false(falsefalsetrue)=true 
lambda_true = fn(x, _) -> x end lambda_false = fn(_, y) -> y end lambda_not = fn(bool) -> bool.(lambda_false,lambda_true) end print = fn(bool) -> IO.puts(bool.("true","false")) end print.(lambda_not.(lambda_true)) #false
print.(lambda_not.(lambda_false)) #true

Já no exemplo acima criamos duas funções true e false que recebem dois parâmetros x e y , a função true retorna somente o parâmetro x e a função false retorna somente o parâmetro y .
Para a negação precisamos criar uma outra função que mapeie uma função booleana, true ou false, em outra função booleana invertida.

Conclusão

Mas qual a função do cálculo lambda no dia à dia? O cálculo lambda é a base do que conhecemos hoje como uma função, entender esse conceito, mesmo que de forma mais superficial, é a porta de entrada para entender outros conceitos de programação funcional.

item.title: XNPV 函数 | XNPV Function
item.description:
获取一系列可能不是周期性的现金​​流的净现值 (NPV) 。

负值被视为付款。

  • 日期：它是对应于付款数组的日期数组。

    折扣率为10

  • 开始日期为 2018 年 6 月 30 日

  • 现金流是在它们对应的确切日期收到的

  • 开始日期和第一笔现金流之间的时间只有 6 个月。

    • 关于 XNPV 的注意事项

    1. 日期中的数字表示为整数
    2. 发生错误时；

    • 值和日期数组的长度不同

    • 任何其他日期早于开始日期

    • 或者

    • The given dates are not recognized by Excel as valid dates. Excel 无法将给定日期识别为有效日期。



    原文:

    Get the Net Present Value (NPV) for a series of cash flows that may not be periodic.

    XNPV uses specific dates that correspond to each cash flow being discounted in the series, whereas the regular** NPV Function** automatically assumes all the time periods are equal.

    XNPV FORMULA

    =XNPV(Rate, Cash Flows, Dates of Cash Flow)

    Rate: The discount rate to be used over the length of the period

    Values: This is an array of numeric values that represent the payments and income where;

    • Negative values are treated as outgoing payment.

    • ** Positive values** are treated as *income *

    Dates: It is an array of dates corresponding to an array of payments.

    XNPV IN Excel

    Assumptions in the XNPV Example

    • The discount rate is 10

    • Start date is June 30, 2018

    • Cash Flow are received on the exact date they correspond to

    • The time between the start date and first cash flow is only 6 months.

    The difference between the XNPV and NPV formulas is that XNPV recognizes that time period between the start date and first cash flow is only 6 months, while the NPV function treats it as a full-time period.

    Things to remember about XNPV

    1. Numbers in dates are indicated as integers
    2. XNPV doesn't discount the initial cash flow
    3. #NUM! error occurs when either;

    • The values and date arrays are of different length

    • Any of the other dates are earlier than the start date

    • #VALUE error - occurs when either;

    • The values or rates arguments are non-numeric; or

    • The given dates are not recognized by Excel as valid dates.

item.title: 在 AWS Amplify 上托管 sveltekit | Hosting sveltekit on AWS Amplify
item.description:
马克框架

发表于如果您使用的是@sveltejs/adapter-static ，这可能会派上用场！

转到 AWS Amplify 项目，在侧栏中单击App settings: Rewrites and redirects 、编辑、打开文本编辑器并粘贴以下内容。

 [ { "source" : "</^[^.]+$| \\ .(?!(svelte|css|gif|ico|jpg|js|png|txt|svg|woff|ttf)$)([^.]+$)/>" , "target" : "/index.html" , "status" : "200" , "condition" : null }, { "source" : "/<*>" , "target" : "/" , "status" : "404-200" , "condition" : null }
]

接下来将<base href="/" /> > 添加到app.html的<head />标记中。

The Amplify Redirect and Rewrite Config and the <base href="/" /> directs all the failed responses back to the index file, making any request that fails fall back to the sveltekit router. Amplify Redirect and Rewrite Config 和<base href="/" />将所有失败的响应引导回索引文件，使任何失败的请求回退到 sveltekit 路由器。

Sarit Chaet Hudis - Sarit Chaet Hudis - May 14 5月14日



原文:

Marc Frame

Posted on May 17

If you are using the @sveltejs/adapter-static this might come in handy!

Go to the AWS Amplify project, in the side bar click App settings: Rewrites and redirects, Edit, Open Text Editor and paste the following.

[ { "source": "</^[^.]+$|\\.(?!(svelte|css|gif|ico|jpg|js|png|txt|svg|woff|ttf)$)([^.]+$)/>", "target": "/index.html", "status": "200", "condition": null }, { "source": "/<*>", "target": "/", "status": "404-200", "condition": null }
]

next add a <base href="/" /> to the <head /> tag in the app.html.

The Amplify Redirect and Rewrite Config and the <base href="/" /> directs all the failed responses back to the index file, making any request that fails fall back to the sveltekit router.

Sarit Chaet Hudis - May 14

item.title: Arquitetura 六角形 | Arquitetura hexagonal
item.description:
• 未来展望：
    限制bem definidos：
    埃斯卡拉：
    例如：Se eu quiser mudar umprovider de dados extras no cadastro ele tem que estar desacoplado para que seja possível mudar facilmente

字体：Elemar Júnior（ https://eximia.co/quais-sao-as-diferencas-entre-arquitetura-e-design-de-software/ ）

科伯恩

观察

  • A arquitetura hexagonal não define padrão de como deve ser criado o software A arquitetura hexagonal não define padrão de como deve ser criado o 软件

  • Apesar de Arquitetura hexagonal, clean arch e arquitetura onion terem o mesmo principio de separar a aplicação da camada de negocio, a principal diferença da arquitetura hexagonal é que ela não define o padrão de como criar o software Apesar de Arquitetura hexagonal, clean arch e arquitetura onion terem o mesmo principio de separar a aplicação da camada de negocio, a aplicação da camada de negocio, a principal diferença da arquitetura hexagonal é que ela não define o padrão de como criar o software

Nitya Narasimhan - Nitya Narasimhan - May 10 5月10日



原文:

  • Visão de futuro:
    A maioria dos softwares é feita pensando no agora e não no futuro

  • Limites bem definidos:
    Não misturar as regras de negócio com framework, não deixar o app fortemente acoplado com bibliotecas terceiras

  • Troca e adição de componentes:
    Tudo que for desenvolvido deve ser facilmente trocado

  • Escala:
    Cada aplicação relacionada (cache, banco de dados, ...etc) deve ficar em seu próprio servidor

  • Otimizações frequentes:
    Todo software vai ter debito técnico mas é preciso sempre otimizar para não deixar um grande debito que vai ser mais complicado de solucionar no futuro

  • Preparado para mudanças:
    Ex: Se eu quiser mudar um provedor de dados extras no cadastro ele tem que estar desacoplado para que seja possível mudar facilmente

Atividades relacionadas a arquitetura de software são sempre de design. Entretanto, nem todas atividade de design são sobre arquitetura. O objetivo primário da arquitetura de software é garantir que os atributos de qualidade, restrições de alto nível e os objetivos do negócio, sejam atendidos pelo sistema. Qualquer decisão de design que não tenha relação com este objetivo não é arquitetural. Todas as decisões de design para um componente que não sejam “visíveis” fora dele, geralmente, também não são.
Fonte: Elemar Júnior (https://eximia.co/quais-sao-as-diferencas-entre-arquitetura-e-design-de-software/)

Design é uma decisão micro enquanto Arquitetura se preocupa com o macro, o Design pode ser exemplo como definir o nome das variveis ou até mesmo SOLID que são regras de design, então dependendo Design pode ou não interferir na arquitetura

Arquitetura Hexagonal

Permite que uma aplicação seja igualmente desenvolvida para os usuários, programas, testes automatizados ou scripts bash, e para ser desenvolvida e testada de forma isolada dos seus eventuais devices e bancos de dados de produção
Cockburn

Perceba que temos uma aplicação no centro e diversos adaptadores para a comunicação externa da aplicação

É como em uma casa onde não ligamos diretamente os equipamentos na rede elétrica da casa e sim em tomadas que nesse caso servem como adaptadores


No lado esquerdo da aplicação temos os clientes e no lado direito temos os servidores, e a ideia é que a aplicação utilize os servidores para responder os clientes, sendo que todos vão estar conectados aos adaptadores

Utilizando os conceitos de inversão de dependências, os clientes vão sempre se comunicar através de interfaces enquanto os bancos de dados por exemplo vão sempre se comunicar com os DataReaders.

Observações

  • A arquitetura hexagonal não define padrão de como deve ser criado o software

  • Apesar de Arquitetura hexagonal, clean arch e arquitetura onion terem o mesmo principio de separar a aplicação da camada de negocio, a principal diferença da arquitetura hexagonal é que ela não define o padrão de como criar o software

Nitya Narasimhan - May 10
item.author: yanpiing

item.title: 我制作了一个 RESTful HTTP 客户端，它会让你的生活更轻松 | I've made a RESTful HTTP client which will make your life much easier
item.description:
利奥维纳

发表于阅读本文的大多数人可能已经熟悉并使用axios从您的应用程序向 API 发出 HTTP 请求，无论是您自己的 API 还是外部 API。

今天我想向你介绍4rest ，这是我在axios之上构建的新 npm 包，旨在帮助你轻松、快速地设置应用程序的所有功能以向 API 发出 HTTP 请求，并使它们井井有条尽可能根据 API 的数据模型将它们拆分为服务。

让我们看一个基本的用法示例：


  1. 首先，您使用 API 基本 URL 和其他相关配置创建一个森林实例。
 import forest from " 4rest " ; export const instance = forest . create ({ axiosSettings : { baseURL : " http://localhost:5000 " } });
  1. 然后我们使用刚刚创建的实例创建Forest Service 。
 import { instance } from " ./forestInstance " ; import { UserWithId , User } from " ./types " ; export const userService = instance . createService < UserWithId , User > ( " user " );

就是这样！
只需执行这 2 个简单的步骤，我们就获得了 9 个不同的函数，用于调用 API，其中包含请求负载和响应数据的类型，包括：

  • getAll
  • getById
  • deleteAll
  • deleteById
  • post
  • patch
  • patchById
  • put
  • putById

让我们看几个在我们的应用程序中使用服务方法的示例：

 // GET http://localhost:5000/user async function getUsers () { const users : User [] = ( await userService . getAll ()). data ; } // GET http://localhost:5000/user/:id async function getUserById ( id : string ) { const user : User = ( await userService . getById ( id )). data ; }
 // POST http://localhost:5000/user async function createUser ( newUser : User ) { const userCreated : User = ( await userService . post ( newUser )). data ; }
 // PATCH http://localhost:5000/user async function updateUser ( partialUser : Partial < User > ) { const updatedUser : User = ( await userService . patch ( partialUser )). data ; } // PATCH http://localhost:5000/user/:id async function updateUserById ( id : ObjectId , partialUser : Partial < User > ) { const updatedUser : User = ( await userService . patchById ( id , partialUser )). data ; }

这只是您如何使用4rest的最基本示例，但还有更多选项可以配置服务和实例，这让您可以做比本文中展示的更多的事情，例如zod有效负载和响应数据类型验证、方法路由配置、自定义具有自定义方法、请求 onSuccess 和 onError 处理等的服务。

看更多：

希望你会发现这个包很有用。
 我想听听您对包装的反馈和对未来改进的建议😀

克里斯蒂安·费尔南多—— Apr 27 4月27日



原文:

LiorVainer

Posted on May 16

Most of you reading this are probably already familiar and using axios for making HTTP requests from your application to an API, whether it is your own API or an external one.

Today I want to introduce you to 4rest, the new npm package I built, on top of axios, which is designed to help you set up all of your app's functions for making HTTP requests to API, easily, quickly, and make them as organized as possible by splitting them to services based on API's data models.

Let's see a basic usage example:


  1. First of all you create a Forest Instance with your API base URL and other relevant configuration. 
import forest from "4rest"; export const instance = forest.create({ axiosSettings: { baseURL: "http://localhost:5000" } });
  1. Then we create a Forest Service using the instance we just made. 
import { instance } from "./forestInstance";
import { UserWithId, User } from "./types"; export const userService = instance.createService<UserWithId, User>("user");

That's It!
Just by doing these 2 simple steps we got our self a total of 9 different functions for making calls to an API with types for request payload and response data on them, including:

  • getAll
  • getById
  • deleteAll
  • deleteById
  • post
  • patch
  • patchById
  • put
  • putById

Let's see a few examples of using the service methods in our app:

// GET http://localhost:5000/user
async function getUsers() { const users: User[] = (await userService.getAll()).data;
} // GET http://localhost:5000/user/:id
async function getUserById(id: string) { const user: User = (await userService.getById(id)).data;
}

// POST http://localhost:5000/user
async function createUser(newUser: User) { const userCreated: User = (await userService.post(newUser)).data;
}

// PATCH http://localhost:5000/user
async function updateUser(partialUser: Partial<User>) { const updatedUser: User = (await userService.patch(partialUser)).data;
} // PATCH http://localhost:5000/user/:id
async function updateUserById(id: ObjectId, partialUser: Partial<User>) { const updatedUser: User = (await userService.patchById(id, partialUser)).data;
}

This is only the most basic example of how you use 4rest but there are a lot more options to configure service and instance, which let you do much more than shown in this article like zod payload and response data type validations, methods routes configuration, custom service with custom methods, requests onSuccess and onError handling and more.

See more:

4rest Full docs and source code on Github

4rest NPM Page

Hope you will find this package useful.
I would like to hear your feedback on the package and suggestions for future improvement 😀

Cristian Fernando - Apr 27
item.author: liorvainer

item.title: 有人可以帮我解决这个代码吗？ | Can someone Help me with this CODE?
item.description:
苏拉夫104k

发表于结构节点 { int 数据； 结构节点*链接；

临时=（结构节点

临时->数据=数据； 临时->链接=NULL； 头=温度； 返回头；

结构节点 *ptr=head; 临时1->数据=数据； 如果（ptr=head）{head->link=temp1； 遍历=头； 遍历=遍历->链接；

结构节点头=NULL； 结构节点 *curr=NULL; 整数节点；

头=（结构节点

头=add_at_empty（头，头->数据）；

节点=节点-1；

 for(int i=0;i<nodes;i++) { std::cout<<"Enter data of next node "<<std::endl; std::cin>>curr->data; add_at_end(head, curr->data); }

头=打印数据（头）；

返回0；
} }



原文:

Sourav104k

Posted on May 17

struct node { int data; struct node *link;

};

struct node add_at_empty(struct node *head, int data) { struct node *temp;

temp= (struct node

)malloc(sizeof(struct node)); temp->data=data; temp->link=NULL; head=temp; return head;

}

struct node add_at_end(struct node *head, int data) { struct node *temp1;

temp1= (struct node

)malloc(sizeof(struct node)); struct node *ptr=head; temp1->data=data; temp1->link=NULL; if(ptr=head) { head->link=temp1; ptr=ptr->link; } else { ptr->link=temp1; ptr=temp1; } return head;

}

struct node *print_data(struct node *head) { struct node *traverse; traverse=head; while(traverse!=NULL) { std::cout<data<<" ";

traverse=traverse->link;

} int main() {

struct node head=NULL; struct node *curr=NULL; int nodes;

head=(struct node

)malloc(sizeof(struct node));

curr=(struct node*)malloc(sizeof(struct node)); std::cout<<"enter Number of nodes required"< std::cin>>nodes; std::cout<<"Enter the data of first node "< std::cin>>head->data; head=add_at_empty(head, head->data);

nodes=nodes-1;

for(int i=0;i<nodes;i++)
 {
     std::cout<<"Enter data of next node "<<std::endl;
     std::cin>>curr->data;
     add_at_end(head, curr->data);
 }

head=print_data(head);

return 0;
}

item.title: 什么是 HTML？ 我作为 Web 开发人员的愿景。 | What is HTML? My Vision as a Web Developer.
item.description:
信息技术领域的许多人都知道 HTML 的首字母缩写词：超文本标记语言，将其声明为一种基于使用标签定义 Web 文档的语言 Word Wide Web 上的系统和应用程序是由什么组成的，但实际上是什么Web 文档对我们开发人员意味着什么？

网络发生了变化，这也带来了网络文档结构的变化，这意味着重要性集中在网站内容的管理以及这些内容所针对的受众上。 为此，我们必须添加一系列元素、编程语言、CSS 框架的出现，以根据需要处理这些通过 Internet 大量吞噬的信息内容来生成用户体验和用户界面。

因此，内容管理变得如此重要，以至于应用于每个 Web 文档的技术和开发对于呈现全球数百万用户访问内容的视觉方式至关重要。 这使我们肩负着确保为这个伟大的世界数字图书馆建立一个有用且可靠的网站的重大责任。

今天，我们的开发人员问自己，我应该使用哪种网络语言进行编程？ 也许是 PHP，也许是 Javascript，或者使用什么 CSS 框架，或者什么 Web 模板最合适，但最大的责任应该始终致力于构建一个负责任的内容 Web，作为一个开发社区，我们有义务在我们采取的每一步中构建它。 通过我们在网络上的数字参与给我们。

推特：@ozzies_code



原文:

Many in the world of information technology know HTML by its acronym: Hyper Text Markup Language to declare it as a language based on the use of tags to define a web document what systems and applications on the Word Wide Web are made of but really What does a web document mean for us developers?

The web has changed and this has brought with it changes in the structure of a web document, which means that the importance is focused on the management of the content of a website and towards the audience to which such content is directed. To this we must add the emergence of a series of elements, programming languages, CSS frameworks to generate a user experience and user interface in accordance with the need to address such information content that is devoured en masse through the Internet.

Hence, content management has become so important that the technologies and development applied to each web document are essential to present the visual way in which the content will be approached by millions of users around the world. Which brings us to the great responsibility of ensuring that we build a useful and reliable website for this great world digital library.

Today we developers ask ourselves in which language for the web should I program? maybe PHP or maybe Javascript or what CSS framework to use or what web template will be the most appropriate but the greatest responsibility should always aim to build a responsible content web that as a development community we are obliged to build with each step that we take. give us through our digital participation on the web.

Twitter: @ozzies_code
item.author: ozziescode

item.title: 音乐星期一——你在听什么？ (May 16) （5月16日） | Music Monday — What are you listening to? (May 16)
item.description:
封面图片来源： sElf - Runaway（音乐视频）

在这个每周系列中，人们可以加入并删除他们正在收听的任何内容的链接 + 浏览其他建议。

如果您喜欢谈论音乐，请考虑关注 org #music 讨论以获取更多关于音乐的对话！

同样值得注意的是，我的朋友@duxtech在这里有这个系列的西班牙语版本，以防你也想插话。 那么，你们今天都在听什么？ 注意：您可以使用以下语法在大多数平台上嵌入指向您歌曲的链接{% embed https://... %} 。

期待听听大家的建议！ 尼克·泰勒（他/他）—— Apr 22 4月22日



原文:

cover image credits: sElf - Runaway (music video)

In this weekly series, folks can chime in and drop links to whatever it is they're listening to + browse others suggestions.

If you like talking about music, consider following the org #music discussions for more conversations about music!

Also noteworthy, my friend @duxtech has a Spanish speaking version of this series here in case you'd like to chime in there as well. 🙌

So, what have y'all been listening to today? Note: you can embed a link to your song on most platforms using the following syntax {% embed https://... %}.

I look forward to listening to y'all's suggestions! 👂 🎶

Nick Taylor (he/him) - Apr 22
item.author: michaeltharrington

item.title: 您在团队页面上寻找什么？ | What do you look for on a team page?
item.description:
我正在向我们公司的网站添加一个团队页面，我想知道：

  • 你能回忆起一个非常可爱/信息丰富/真棒的团队页面示例吗？
  • 你在这样的页面上寻找什么信息？ 有帮助吗？
  • 你通常什么时候查看团队页面？

谢谢！

就我而言，我记得React 团队页面帮助我完成了我的 OSS 贡献，因为我注意到他们只是有爱好的人😂

我还记得访问UPchieve 的团队页面并在我在那里采访时“了解”组成团队的人。 我查看了技能分布和角色，以及人口统计数据。 我想想象考虑到不同工作组的规模，在那里工作会是什么样子。

(not relevant but while I decided to take another offer, I think about the UPchieve interviewing experience only fondly) （不相关，但当我决定接受另一个报价时，我只是深情地想到了 UPchieve 的面试经历）



原文:

I am working on adding a team page to our company's website and I'm wondering:

  • can you recall an example of a team page that'd just really lovely/informative/awesome?
  • what information do you look for on such a page? is it helpful at all?
  • when do you typically check team page?

Thank you!

From my side, I remember that React team page helped me with my OSS contributions because I noticed they are just humans with hobbies 😂

I also remember visiting UPchieve's team page and "getting to know" the folks who make the team when I interviewed there. I looked at the skills distribution and roles, as well as the demographics. I wanted to imagine what working there could be like given the size of different working groups.

(not relevant but while I decided to take another offer, I think about the UPchieve interviewing experience only fondly)
item.author: sylwiavargas

item.title: 为什么辞职文章如此受欢迎？ | Why quitting job articles are so popular?
item.description:
霍孔 M.

发表于我们经常看到有人发帖说要辞职； 我的意思是包括所有博客，而不仅仅是 dev.to。 不知何故，他们设法吸引了很多关注。 你对这个话题有什么看法？ 是不是大多数人讨厌他们的工作，但没有勇气离开它，所以他们在此类博客或其他东西中寻求灵感？

让我们知道您对此的看法。 让我们就这个话题进行一次有意义的讨论。

萨尔玛·阿拉姆-奈勒 - May 11 5月11日



原文:

Khokon M.

Posted on May 16

We often see people posting about quitting their job; I mean including all blogs and not just dev.to. And somehow, they manage to attract a lot of attention. What are your thoughts on this topic? Is it like most people hate their job but don't have the courage to leave it so they seek inspiration in such blogs or something else?

Let us know know your thoughts on this. Let's have a meaningful discussion on this topic.

Salma Alam-Naylor - May 11
item.author: khokon

item.title: Neovim 的自动补全 | Autocompletion for Neovim
item.description:
哈立德·纳萨尔

发表于自动补全可以让您使用 NeoVim 提高工作效率，其中一项最酷的功能是自动补全，因此我们有一些插件可以根据性能在它们之间进行选择

这些插件很好用，但由于 Javascript 和 Python，它们非常繁重，所以我决定改用另一种解决方案
解决方案是名为nvim-cmp的 Lua 插件
使用此插件，您可以以最低的性能为您的 neovim 添加自动完成功能，您所要做的就是下载语言服务器

安装

在安装插件之前你需要先安装 Lua

你需要一个插件管理器，你可以选择

  • 病原
  • 文德尔

安装 nvim-cmp 插件后，您必须安装一些外部插件

" ~/.config/nvim/init.nvim Plug 'hrsh7th/cmp-nvim-lsp' Plug 'hrsh7th/cmp-buffer' Plug 'hrsh7th/cmp-path' Plug 'hrsh7th/cmp-cmdline' Plug 'hrsh7th/nvim-cmp' Plug 'hrsh7th/vim-vsnip'

安装您的语言的服务器后，您可能需要一些 Lua 代码来启用它
因此您可以使用此代码复制并粘贴到您的init.nvim文件中
另外，不要忘记查看此页面以获取适合您语言的服务器

就我而言，我将安装rust-analyzer

 $ sudo pacman -S rust-analyzer
 " ~/.config/nvim/init.nvim lua << EOF -- Setup nvim - cmp . local cmp = require 'cmp' cmp . setup ({ snippet = { -- REQUIRED - you must specify a snippet engine expand = function ( args ) vim . fn [ "vsnip#anonymous" ]( args . body ) -- For `vsnip` users . -- require ( 'luasnip' ). lsp_expand ( args . body ) -- For `luasnip` users . -- require ( 'snippy' ). expand_snippet ( args . body ) -- For `snippy` users . -- vim . fn [ "UltiSnips#Anon" ]( args . body ) -- For `ultisnips` users . end , }, mapping = { [ '<Cb>' ] = cmp . mapping ( cmp . mapping . scroll_docs ( -4 ), { 'i' , 'c' }), [ '<Cf>' ] = cmp . mapping ( cmp . mapping . scroll_docs ( 4 ), { 'i' , 'c' }), [ '<C-Space>' ] = cmp . mapping ( cmp . mapping . complete (), { 'i' , 'c' }), [ '<Cy>' ] = cmp . config . disable , -- Specify ` cmp . config . disable` if you want to remove the default ` < C - y > ` mapping . [ '<Ce>' ] = cmp . mapping ({ i = cmp . mapping . abort (), c = cmp . mapping . close (), }), [ '<CR>' ] = cmp . mapping . confirm ({ select = true }), -- Accept currently selected item . Set `select` to `false` to only confirm explicitly selected items . }, sources = cmp . config . sources ({ { name = 'nvim_lsp' }, { name = 'vsnip' }, -- For vsnip users . -- { name = 'luasnip' }, -- For luasnip users . -- { name = 'ultisnips' }, -- For ultisnips users . -- { name = 'snippy' }, -- For snippy users . }, { { name = 'buffer' }, }) }) -- Set configuration for specific filetype . cmp . setup . filetype ( 'gitcommit' , { sources = cmp . config . sources ({ { name = 'cmp_git' }, -- You can specify the `cmp_git` source if you were installed it . }, { { name = 'buffer' }, }) }) -- Use buffer source for `/` ( if you enabled `native_menu` , this won' t work anymore ). cmp . setup . cmdline ( '/' , { sources = { { name = 'buffer' } } }) -- Use cmdline & path source for ':' ( if you enabled `native_menu` , this won' t work anymore ). cmp . setup . cmdline ( ':' , { sources = cmp . config . sources ({ { name = 'path' } }, { { name = 'cmdline' } }) }) -- Setup lspconfig . local capabilities = require ( 'cmp_nvim_lsp' ). update_capabilities ( vim . lsp . protocol . make_client_capabilities ()) -- Replace < YOUR_LSP_SERVER > with each lsp server you' ve enabled . require 'lspconfig' . rust_analyzer . setup { capabilities = capabilities , } EOF

您可以使用 CTRL+B 或 CTRL+F 滚动代码建议

亚历克斯马尔多纳多索托 - May 3 5月3日



原文:

Khaled Nassar

Posted on May 16

One of the coolest features that can make you more productive with NeoVim is autocompletion, so we have some plugins to choose between them based on the performance

these plugins are great to use but they are very heavy because of Javascript and Python, so I decided to switch to another solution
and the solution is Lua Plugin called nvim-cmp
with this plugin you can add an auto-completion to your neovim with minimal performance, all you have to do is download the language server

Install

before installing the plugins you need to install Lua first

and you need a Plugin manager you can choose between

  • vim-plug ( I use this )
  • Pathogen
  • Vundle

after installing the nvim-cmp plugin you have to install some external plugins

" ~/.config/nvim/init.nvim Plug 'hrsh7th/cmp-nvim-lsp' Plug 'hrsh7th/cmp-buffer' Plug 'hrsh7th/cmp-path' Plug 'hrsh7th/cmp-cmdline' Plug 'hrsh7th/nvim-cmp' Plug 'hrsh7th/vim-vsnip'

after installing the server of your language you may need some Lua code to enable it
so you can use this code to copy & paste into your init.nvim file
Also, Don't forget to check this page to get the right server for your language

in my case I'll install rust-analyzer

$ sudo pacman -S rust-analyzer

" ~/.config/nvim/init.nvim
lua <<EOF -- Setup nvim-cmp. local cmp = require'cmp' cmp.setup({ snippet = { -- REQUIRED - you must specify a snippet engine expand = function(args) vim.fn["vsnip#anonymous"](args.body) -- For `vsnip` users. -- require('luasnip').lsp_expand(args.body) -- For `luasnip` users. -- require('snippy').expand_snippet(args.body) -- For `snippy` users. -- vim.fn["UltiSnips#Anon"](args.body) -- For `ultisnips` users. end, }, mapping = { ['<C-b>'] = cmp.mapping(cmp.mapping.scroll_docs(-4), { 'i', 'c' }), ['<C-f>'] = cmp.mapping(cmp.mapping.scroll_docs(4), { 'i', 'c' }), ['<C-Space>'] = cmp.mapping(cmp.mapping.complete(), { 'i', 'c' }), ['<C-y>'] = cmp.config.disable, -- Specify `cmp.config.disable` if you want to remove the default `<C-y>` mapping. ['<C-e>'] = cmp.mapping({ i = cmp.mapping.abort(), c = cmp.mapping.close(), }), ['<CR>'] = cmp.mapping.confirm({ select = true }), -- Accept currently selected item. Set `select` to `false` to only confirm explicitly selected items. }, sources = cmp.config.sources({ { name = 'nvim_lsp' }, { name = 'vsnip' }, -- For vsnip users. -- { name = 'luasnip' }, -- For luasnip users. -- { name = 'ultisnips' }, -- For ultisnips users. -- { name = 'snippy' }, -- For snippy users. }, { { name = 'buffer' }, }) }) -- Set configuration for specific filetype. cmp.setup.filetype('gitcommit', { sources = cmp.config.sources({ { name = 'cmp_git' }, -- You can specify the `cmp_git` source if you were installed it. }, { { name = 'buffer' }, }) }) -- Use buffer source for `/` (if you enabled `native_menu`, this won't work anymore). cmp.setup.cmdline('/', { sources = { { name = 'buffer' } } }) -- Use cmdline & path source for ':' (if you enabled `native_menu`, this won't work anymore). cmp.setup.cmdline(':', { sources = cmp.config.sources({ { name = 'path' } }, { { name = 'cmdline' } }) }) -- Setup lspconfig. local capabilities = require('cmp_nvim_lsp').update_capabilities(vim.lsp.protocol.make_client_capabilities()) -- Replace <YOUR_LSP_SERVER> with each lsp server you've enabled. require'lspconfig'.rust_analyzer.setup{ capabilities = capabilities, }

EOF

You can scroll in the code suggestion with CTRL+B or CTRL+F

AlexMaldonadoSoto - May 3
item.author: knassar702

item.title: 在 React 项目中设置 Jest 和 React 测试库 | 分步指南 | Setup Jest and React Testing Library in a React project | a step-by-step guide
item.description:
不久前，我遇到了测试的概念，特别是“编写测试”。 我过去常常通过运行打开浏览器并手动执行所有操作来测试所有内容，但是当您更改某些内容时，您不知道会出现什么问题，而且通常会出现问题。 关键是真正的应用程序需要测试以确保我们的功能不会意外中断。 在本教程中，我将引导您完成设置 Jest 和 React 测试库 (RTL) 以测试 React 应用程序的过程

注意：这是从头开始设置 React 环境系列的第三篇文章。 在第一篇文章中，我们从头开始创建了一个全新的 React 项目，没有使用create-react-app ，第二篇文章我们配置了 ESLint、Prettier 和 Husky，我们将根据这个进度来设置 Jest 和 RTL 并编写我们的第一个测试.

先决条件

  • 我将假设您正在运行一个 react 应用程序以及我们从以前的文章中构建的所有内容，如果您需要这篇文章用于其他目的，您也可以继续关注，但请注意它可能看起来与我所拥有的不同
  • 这就是你所需要的，让我们开始吧

    为什么要测试？
    在某些情况下，测试编写起来可能很无聊，而且毫无用处，但我不能过分强调对应用程序进行广泛测试的重要性。 添加新代码后如何确保您的应用程序仍然可以运行？ 通过写作测试。 建议您测试您编写的所有内容，以确保您的应用程序按预期运行。 许多组织都非常严格地执行测试，有些组织使用测试驱动开发，在实现功能之前编写测试

    笑话
    它有许多内置的快照测试、函数模拟、覆盖率收集，通常易于配置和使用。 在这个配置中，我们将使用 Jest 运行我们编写的所有测试，知道哪个测试失败或通过并收集覆盖率，即告诉我们代码库中未覆盖/测试的行。 在此处了解有关 Jest 的更多信息

    反应测试库
    如前所述，官方 React 文档建议使用 RTL 来启用和鼓励编写测试，以像最终用户一样使用您的组件。 在此处了解有关 RTL 的更多信息

    在我们的配置中，我们将同时使用 Jest 和 RTL，但请注意，任何一个都可以单独使用，也可以与其他工具一起使用。 为了更好的测试，我们将使用 React 测试库来查找我们的组件并操作它们，而 Jest 将确定通过和失败的测试以及测试覆盖率

    本指南将更多地介绍配置，因此我不会过多介绍如何编写测试。 有关测试 React 应用程序的更多信息，请在此处查看这篇精彩的文章

    话不多说。 让我们按照以下步骤开始这个派对
    从以前的文章这里是当前的

    运行以下命令安装 RTL 依赖项（作为开发依赖项）

 npm install --save-dev @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom

如果你更喜欢纱线

yarn add --dev @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom
  • 匹配器将使您的测试更具声明性，更易于阅读和维护。 稍后会详细介绍

运行以下命令将 jest 安装为开发依赖项

npm install --save-dev jest jest-environment-jsdom

如果你更喜欢纱线

yarn add --dev jest jest-environment-jsdom
  • 您可以通过在 package.json 中添加 jest 条目来配置 Jest，或者在根文件夹中添加名为jest.config.js的文件。 为了保持 package.json 干净，我们将使用jest.config.js

    在根文件夹中创建一个名为jest.config.js的文件并添加以下代码配置。

    大多数 jest 配置默认配置良好，但您可以通过在此文件中添加更多字段来自定义所有内容。 在此处了解有关所有配置的更多信息

    module . export = { collectCoverage : true , collectCoverageFrom : [ ' src/**/*.{js,jsx} ' ], coverageDirectory : ' coverage ' , testEnvironment : ' jsdom ' , }

    了解这个配置

    • 在根文件夹中创建一个名为jest.setup.js的文件 输入以下代码行
    import ' @testing-library/jest-dom '

    这意味着我们正在从@testing-library/jest-dom包中导入所有内容

    • 在我们之前创建的jest.config.js文件中添加setupFilesAfterEnv的另一个字段并将其值设置为['<rootDir>/jest.setup.js']这将告诉 jest 我们编写的每个测试它将从jest.setup.js加载配置jest.setup.js即使用 React 测试库你的jest.config.js应该是这样的
    modules . export = { collectCoverage : true , collectCoverageFrom : [ ' src/**/*.{js,jsx} ' ], coverageDirectory : ' coverage ' , testEnvironment : ' jsdom ' , setupFilesAfterEnv : [ ' <rootDir>/jest.setup.js ' ] }

    在第二篇文章中，我们设置了 ESLint，默认情况下，如果你使用 Jest，Eslint 会出错，因为我们不导入我们在 Jest 中使用的一些函数，并且 eslint 不接受遵循以下步骤

    • 运行以下命令安装eslint-plugin-jest ，这将使 Eslint 识别 Jest 代码
    npm install --save-dev eslint-plugin-jest
     yarn add --dev eslint-plugin-jest
    • 在eslintrc.json的 plugins 数组中添加"jest"
    • 在eslintrc.json添加"plugin:jest/recommended",在extends中使用推荐的 jest 语法
    • 在env条目的eslintrc.json中添加"jest/globals": true

    您的eslintrc.json最终应该看起来像这样

    { " env " : { " browser " : true , " es2021 " : true , " jest/globals " : true }, " extends " : [ " plugin:react/recommended " , " plugin:jest/recommended " , " airbnb " , " prettier " ], " parserOptions " : { " ecmaFeatures " : { " jsx " : true }, " ecmaVersion " : " latest " , " sourceType " : " module " }, " plugins " : [ " react " , " jest " ], " rules " : { " no-underscore-dangle " : 0 , " import/extensions " : [ " error " , " ignorePackages " , { " js " : " always " , " jsx " : " always " } ] } }

    在 script 对象的package.json中添加以下脚本

    scripts :{ ... //scripts you already have test : " jest " , coverage : " jest --coverage " }

    这就是所有配置，现在您可以编写一些测试

    编写测试
    通过对流，我们在您要测试的文件的文件夹中创建一个名为test或__test__的文件夹，并且测试的名称为foo.test.js或bar.test.js

    • 在src文件夹中创建一个test文件夹并添加App.test.jsx来测试App.jsx和以下代码
    import { render , screen } from ' @testing-library/react ' ; import React from ' react ' ; import App from ' ../App.jsx ' ; describe ( ' App tests ' , () => { it ( ' should contains the heading 1 ' , () => { render ( < App /> ); const heading = screen . getByText ( /Hello world! I am using React/i ); expect ( heading ). toBeInTheDocument () }); });
    • 通过运行npm run test and it should pass

    在这个测试中，我们正在测试我们有文本Hello world! I am using React 这就是我们设置 Jest 和 React 测试库来测试 React 应用程序的方式

    有关本文中提到的代码的参考，请查看此GitHub 存储库



原文:

Not long ago that I came across with the concept of testing specifically "writing tests". I used to test everything by running opening browsers and doing everything manually but you can't know what can go wrong when you change something and usually it does. The point is that real apps need testing to ensure that our features will not break unexpectedly. In this tutorial I will walk you through in the process of setting up Jest and React testing library (RTL) for testing React applications

Note: this is a third article in the series of setting up a React environment from scratch. in the first article, we created a brand new React project from scratch without using create-react-app, the second article we configured ESLint, Prettier and Husky and we will base on this progress to setup a Jest and RTL and write our first test.

Prerequisites

  • I will assume that you have a react app running and everything we built from previous articles, you can also follow along if you need this article for other purposes but note that it may look different than what I have
  • VS code: I will be using Vs code as our code editor but feel free to use any of your preference

that's all you need let's get started

Why testing?
Tests can be boring to write and useless in some cases but I can't stress enough the importance of testing extensively your application. how you ensure that your app still works after adding new code? => you write tests, how do you spot bugs that you never though they existed? by writing test. it is recommended that you test everything you write to have confidence that your app is running as expected. Testing is very strictly enforced at many organizations and some use the Test-driven development where tests are written before you implement features

Jest
Jest is an open source test framework created by Facebook and is well integrated with React. it have many built-in like snapshot testing, function mocking, coverage collection and is usually easy to configure and use. in this configuration we will be using Jest to run all tests we write know which one failed or passed and collect coverage i.e. tell us lines that are not covered/tested in our codebase. learn more about Jest here

React Testing Library
React testing library (RTL) is a lightweight is a testing Library that enable us to test React by simulating how users will interact with our application. as mentioned Here the official React documentation recommends using RTL to enable and encourage writing tests that use your components as the end users do. learn more about RTL here

in our configuration we will be using both Jest and RTL but note that any can be used on it's own or with other tools. for better testing we will be using React testing Library to find our components and manipulate them while Jest will determine passing and failing tests and testing coverage

This guide will be more of configuration so I won't cover much about writing tests. for more about testing React application check this great article here

Enough with the talking. let's get this party started follow the following steps
from the previous articles here is the current

1. Install React testing library dependencies

  • run the following command to install RTL dependencies (as dev dependencies) 
npm install --save-dev @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom

if you prefer yarn

yarn add --dev @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom
  • @testing-library/react: the core dependency that install react testing library.
  • @testing-library/jest-dom: allow us to use custom jest matchers to extend jest with react testing library. there matchers will make your tests more declarative, clear to read and to maintain. More on this later

2. Install Jest

  • run the following command to install jest as a dev dependency 
npm install --save-dev jest jest-environment-jsdom

if you prefer yarn

yarn add --dev jest jest-environment-jsdom
  • jest: the core dependency required for Jest to work
  • jest-environment-jsdom: this will allow us to use jsdom and we will use it together with @testing-library/jest-dom

3. Configure Jest
You can configure Jest by adding jest entry in the package.json or add a file named jest.config.js in the root folder. To keep package.json clean we will use jest.config.js

create a file named jest.config.js in the root folder and add the following code configuration.

Most of jest configured are well configured by default but you can customize everything by adding more fields in this file. Learn more about all configurations here

module.export = { collectCoverage: true, collectCoverageFrom: ['src/**/*.{js,jsx}'], coverageDirectory: 'coverage', testEnvironment: 'jsdom',
}

understand this configuration

  • collectCoverage: enables collecting coverage
  • collectCoverageFrom specifies files to collect coverage from this will be from files files in all .js and jsx from src folder
  • coverageDirectory specifies folder jest will put coverage files
  • testEnvironment The test environment that will be used for testing note that we are setting it to jsdom and this will be coming from @testing-library/jest-dom and jest-environment-jsdom packages we installed earlier.

4.Integrate Jest with React testing Library

  • in the root folder create a file named jest.setup.jsenter the following line of code 
import '@testing-library/jest-dom'

this means that we are importing everything from @testing-library/jest-dom package

  • in the jest.config.js file we created earlier add another field of setupFilesAfterEnv and set it's value to be ['<rootDir>/jest.setup.js'] this will tell jest for every test we write it will load configuration from jest.setup.js i.e. use React testing libraly your jest.config.js should look like this 
modules.export = { collectCoverage: true, collectCoverageFrom: ['src/**/*.{js,jsx}'], coverageDirectory: 'coverage', testEnvironment: 'jsdom', setupFilesAfterEnv: ['<rootDir>/jest.setup.js']
}

5.Integrate Jest with ESLint
In the second article we setup ESLint and by default if you use Jest Eslint will give errors because we don't import some functions that we use in Jest and eslint doesn't accept that follow the following steps

  • Run the following command to install eslint-plugin-jest which will make Eslint recognise Jest code 
npm install --save-dev eslint-plugin-jest

yarn add --dev eslint-plugin-jest
  • in the eslintrc.json add "jest" in the plugins array
  • in the eslintrc.json add "plugin:jest/recommended", in the extends to use recommended jest syntax
  • in the eslintrc.json in the env entry add "jest/globals": true

Your eslintrc.json should end up looking like this

{ "env": { "browser": true, "es2021": true, "jest/globals": true }, "extends": [ "plugin:react/recommended", "plugin:jest/recommended", "airbnb", "prettier" ], "parserOptions": { "ecmaFeatures": { "jsx": true }, "ecmaVersion": "latest", "sourceType": "module" }, "plugins": ["react", "jest"], "rules": { "no-underscore-dangle": 0, "import/extensions": [ "error", "ignorePackages", { "js": "always", "jsx": "always" } ] }
}

6. Adding testing scripts
in the package.json in the script object add the following scripts

scripts:{
... //scripts you already have
test: "jest",
coverage: "jest --coverage"
}

test: "jest": will find all our test to which whicha passing and failing
coverage: "jest --coverage": will run our tests too and also collect our coverage

That's all the configuration now you can write some tests

Writing tests
By convection we create a folder called test or __test__ in the folder you have files you want to test and tests will have name foo.test.js or bar.test.js

  • in the src folder create a test folder and add App.test.jsx to test App.jsx and the following code 
import { render, screen } from '@testing-library/react';
import React from 'react';
import App from '../App.jsx'; describe('App tests', () => { it('should contains the heading 1', () => { render(<App />); const heading = screen.getByText(/Hello world! I am using React/i); expect(heading).toBeInTheDocument() });
});
  • run test by running npm run test and it should pass

in this test we are testing that we have text Hello world! I am using React in our page and this should pass as that's the text we used in article 1

There you have it that's how we setup Jest and React Testing Library to test React applications

For reference of code mentioned in this article check this GitHub repository
item.author: yvad60

item.title: 成为远程公司成功开发人员的 7 个技巧 | 7 Tips to be a Successful Developer in a Remote Company
item.description:
当大流行在 2020 年 3 月来袭时，大多数公司被迫远程办公，数百万工人不得不学习如何在家工作。 两年后，很多人现在更喜欢在家工作的选择，许多求职者正在寻找仅限远程工作的公司。

对于软件开发人员来说，在家工作并且仍然有效地作为一个团队工作以产生高质量的结果有很多好处。 但是，您如何在偏远的公司取得成功呢？

在本文中，我将提供 7 个技巧，帮助您在远程公司成为一名成功的开发人员。

沟通是关键

现在，与您的团队进行有效沟通比以往任何时候都更加重要。 现在每个人都在家工作，而不是在一个实体办公室，您必须非常有意识地与队友联系并分享您在项目上的进展。 在实体办公室中，您整天都被其他同事包围，这使您更容易走到某人的办公室并分享您一直在做的事情。 它还使全天与其他团队成员进行临时会议变得更加容易。

但是在偏远的环境中，很容易变得孤立，并整天忙于工作而不接触任何人。 这对您的团队来说是一个问题，因为现在他们不再了解您为项目做出贡献的最新更改。

我的建议是在你工作的一天中定期发布更新。 如果您的公司正在使用 Slack 或其他聊天服务器之类的东西，请确保发布您在过去几个小时内所做的详细更新。

这是我将发布给我的团队的状态更新之一的示例。

状态更新：

  • 完成对 xyz 案例研究的更改并提交审核
  • 审查了几个 PR
  • 对主页进行可访问性审核并致力于提高性能分数

我通常会在中午发布此消息，以向团队更新我一直在做的事情。 我还尝试全天提出问题，以确保我不会在某个特定问题上被阻止太久。 这种沟通方式帮助我与团队保持联系，并帮助我的经理了解我在项目中的位置。

在家里找一个单独的空间来工作

当您在办公室工作时，工作和家庭之间有明显的区别。 这样可以更轻松地结束一天并将工作留在办公室。 但随着远程工作，有时工作和家庭之间的这些界限变得有点模糊。 这就是为什么在家中指定一个单独的工作空间很重要的原因。

在我的情况下，我在另一个房间里有一张小桌子，我可以在那里完成所有工作。 该空间仅用于周一至周五的工作目的。 我发现，通过创造一个工作空间，它可以让我活在当下并完全专注于我的职责。 然后在一天结束时，我退出所有内容并离开工作空间，就像我离开实体企业一样。 这让我可以在不工作时在家中自由放松，并为第二天充电。

我了解每个人的生活情况都不一样，您可能没有指定工作区的空间。 在这些情况下，尽你所能在你的生活环境中为工作腾出一些空间。 你越能将家庭和企业分开，你的工作效率就越高。

设定一致的工作时间

当你在办公室工作时，你已经设定了你要去那里的时间。 可能是 9-5 或 8-3，全天休息。 但是，当您在家工作时，很容易在没有固定时间表的情况下工作，并在标准工作时间之外投入更多时间。 这可能很快导致倦怠，这是开发人员社区中的一个大问题。

设置一致的工作时间表非常重要，这样您的团队才能知道您何时有空，何时下班。 这也迫使你在一个时间框架内工作，这将导致更好的专注、时间管理和生产力。

我个人发现，将时间安排在 8 点到 5 点之间并有休息时间有助于我更好地安排我的一天并完成更多工作，而不是我有一个开放的时间表。

全天休息

开发者健康是社区中的另一个大问题。 许多开发人员，包括我自己在内，已经花了好几个小时在他们的电脑前弯腰试图让某些东西工作。 在这些情况下，很容易忘记时间并出现背部、颈部、肩部和眼睛问题。

在家工作的主要优势之一是能够休息并离开工作空间。 全天休息十分钟，确保四处走走、伸展身体、到户外去喝水。 从电脑上休息会让你的身体和大脑有机会休息和充电。

当我第一次开始远程工作时，我会一次粘在电脑上几个小时。 但我很快就感受到了对健康的负面影响，并开始变得更加有意识地休息。 现在，我已经形成了一种更健康的工作环境，并且可以看到我正在制作的工作质量的差异。

重要会议着装得体

我们都喜欢在家工作时经常伴随的轻松着装要求。 在办公室里，你通常会穿着更适合工作的着装，尤其是在与客户会面时。 但是当你在家的时候，穿一些运动裤和衬衫很容易工作。

如果那天你没有任何会议，那么穿着休闲装就可以了。 但是，如果您要与客户会面或进行远程面试，则着装得体很重要。 请记住，这仍然是一项业务，即使在远程环境中，您也希望尽可能专业。

避免混合个人任务和工作任务

当您在办公室时，您不必担心诸如洗衣服或倒垃圾之类的个人琐事。 但是，当您在家工作时，很容易分心并将个人任务与工作任务混为一谈。 当你工作时，你必须专注于工作，这一点非常重要。 然后在休息期间，您可以处理个人物品。

在我的情况下，我确保完成我正在处理的任务，然后休息一下，穿上一大堆衣服或处理另一项个人琐事。 有时肯定会出现问题，我会确保与团队沟通，以便他们知道我将在一段时间内无法使用。 但只要尽力将个人任务和问题与工作任务分开即可。

时间管理和安排你的一天

为了高效地工作，尽可能地计划好你的一天并相应地安排你的时间是很重要的。 例如，当我今天写这个博客时，我确保留出时间来写初稿，修改它并提交给审查。 在那段时间里，我不专注于其他公关或其他工作职责。 这可确保我 100% 专注于撰写高质量的文章。

完成一项大任务后，我一定要休息一下，补充水分，四处走走，吃点东西。 这让我能够为我一天中的下一个任务注入活力并做好准备。 我发现将我的一天分解为可管理的任务让我有机会专注并完成出色的工作。

结论

这些是我在远程公司成为一名成功的开发人员所需要具备的 7 个技巧。 我鼓励您尝试这些技巧中的每一个，看看它如何影响您的工作效率。

如需 React、Angular、Vue、Web 组件、GraphQL、Node、Bazel 或 Polymer 方面的专家架构指导、培训或咨询，请访问thisdot.co



原文:

When the pandemic hit in March of 2020, most companies were forced to go remote and millions of workers had to learn how to work from home. Two years later, a lot people now prefer the work from home option, and many job seekers are looking for remote-only companies to work for.

For software developers, there are many advantages to working from home and still effectively working as a team to produce high quality results. But how can you be successful in a remote company?

In this article, I will provide 7 tips on what it takes to be a successful developer in a remote company.

Communication is key

It is now more important than ever to communicate effectively with your team. Now that everyone is working from home instead of being in one physical office, you have to be very conscious to reach out to teammates and share your progress on a project. In a physical office, you are surrounded by other co-workers all day, which makes it easier to walk over to someone's office and share what you have been working on. It also makes it easier to have impromptu meetings with other team members throughout the day.

But in a remote setting, it is very easy to become isolated and get carried away with work without reaching out to anyone all day. This becomes an issue for your team, because now they are no longer keeping up to date on the latest changes you have been contributing to the project.

My tip would be to post periodic updates throughout the day of what you have been working on. If your company is using something like Slack or another chat server, make sure to post a detailed update on what you have been up to the past few hours.

Here is an example of one of the status updates I would post to my team.

Status update:

  • finished making changes to xyz case study and put it up for review
  • reviewed a couple of PRs
  • ran accessibility audits for the homepage and working on increasing the performance score

I would usually post this in the middle of the day to update the team on what I have been working on. I also try to ask questions throughout the day to ensure that I don't get blocked on a particular issue for to long. This method of communication helps me stay connected with the team and helps my manager know where I am at in the project.

Find a separate space in your home just for work

When you are working in an office, there is a clear separation between work and home. This makes it easier to end your day and leave your work at the office. But with remote work, sometimes these lines between work and home become a little bit blurry. This is why it is important to designate a separate work space in your home.

In my situation, I have a small desk in another room where I do all of my work. That space is only used Monday through Friday for work purposes. I have found that by creating a work space, it allows me to be in the moment and fully concentrate on my duties. Then at the end of the day, I logout out of everything and leave the work space just like I would leave a physical business. That has given me freedom to relax in my home when I am not working and fully recharge for the next day.

I understand that everyone's living situation is different, and you might not have room for a designated work area. In those cases, do the best you can to carve out some space in your living situation just for work. The more you can do to separate home and business, the more productive you will be at work.

Set consistent work hours

When you are working in an office, you have set hours for when you are going to be there. It might be 9-5 or 8-3 with breaks throughout the day. But when you are working from home, it is very easy to work without a set schedule and put in more time outside of the standard work hours. This can quickly lead to burnout which is a huge issue within the developer community.

It is really important to set a consistent work schedule so your team knows when you are available, and when you are off work. This also forces you to work within a time frame which will lead to better focus, time management and productivity.

I have personally found that setting that time frame from 8-5 with breaks helps me structure my day better and get more done than if I had an open schedule.

Take breaks throughout the day

Developer health is another huge issue within the community. Many developers, including myself have gone for hours hunched over their computers trying to get something to work. In these situations, it is very easy to lose track of time and develop back, neck, shoulder, and eye problems.

One of the key advantages of working from home is being able to take breaks and leave your work space. Take ten minute breaks throughout the day and make sure to walk around, stretch, go outside, and drink water. Taking breaks from the computer will give your body and brain a chance to rest and recharge.

When I first started working remotely, I would be glued to the computer for hours at a time. But I quickly felt the negative health effects and started becoming more intentional about taking breaks. Now, I have developed a healthier work situation and can see the difference in the quality of work I am producing.

Dress appropriately for important meetings

We all enjoy the relaxed dress code that often accompanies working from home. In an office, you will typically be in a more work appropriate attire especially when it comes to meetings with clients. But when you are at home, it is very easy to work in some sweatpants and a t shirt.

If you don't have any meetings that day, then coding in your casual attire is fine. But if you are meeting with clients or interviewing remotely, it is important to dress appropriately. Remember that it is still a business and you want to be as professional as possible, even in a remote setting.

Avoid mixing personal tasks and work tasks

When you are in an office, you don't worry about personal chores like doing laundry or taking the trash out. But when you are working from home, it is very easy to get distracted and mix personal tasks with work tasks. It is very important that while you are working, you are focused on work. Then during breaks, you can take care of personal items.

In my situation, I make sure to finish up the task I was working on and then take a break to put a load of laundry on or take care of another personal chore. There are definitely times where something has come up and I will make sure to communicate that with the team so they know I will be unavailable for a certain amount of time. But just try to do the best that you can separating personal tasks and issues from your work tasks.

Time management and structuring your day

In order to work productively, it is important to plan out your day as best you can and structure your time accordingly. For example, as I am writing this blog today, I made sure to set aside time for writing the first draft, revising it and submitting it for review. During that time block, I am not focused on other PR's or other work responsibilities. That ensures I am focused 100% on writing a high quality article.

Once I complete a large task, then I make sure to take a break, hydrate, walk around and eat something. That allows me to energize and gear up for the next task in my day. I found that breaking up my day into manageable tasks has provided me the opportunity to focus and produce good work.

Conclusion

Those are my 7 tips on what it takes to be a successful developer in a remote company. I encourage you to give each of those tips a try and see how it affects your work productivity.

This Dot Labs is a modern web consultancy focused on helping companies realize their digital transformation efforts. For expert architectural guidance, training, or consulting in React, Angular, Vue, Web Components, GraphQL, Node, Bazel, or Polymer, visit thisdot.co
item.author: codergirl1991

item.title: 在 Postgres 应用程序上开始使用 PostGIS | Getting Started with PostGIS on Postgres App
item.description:
如果您以前处理过数据，那么您可能听说过 Postgres，但如果您没有听说过，Postgres 是一个领先的开源关系数据库，始于 30 多年前。 在带有开源版本的 14 版上，通过下载Postgres 应用程序可以非常容易地在 Mac 上设置项目。 您不必获取该应用程序，您可以只下载您想要的内容，但如果您想要一种快速开始使用 Postgis 进行地理空间分析的方法，请继续获取该应用程序，其中包含 postgis 所需的文件。

什么是PostGIS ？ 它是 Postgres 的空间扩展，它允许您存储point和polygon等数据类型，而不是尝试将坐标存储为小数。 然后，您可以直接从数据库中进行地理空间分析。 例如，如果您有一个地理区域并且想要查找中心，则可以使用ST_Centroid()对单个几何图形或整个列运行查询。

您甚至可以在数据库中开发地理编码器。 我将在这篇文章的后续文章中深入探讨这一点。

那么，一旦你安装了 Postgres 应用程序，你如何设置 Postgis？ 首先，创建一个新数据库（不要在 Postgres 数据库中这样做）。

 CREATE DATABASE geocoder;

在您向用户授予所有访问权限和特权后，您需要做的就是进入启用 Postgis 的数据库，然后进入数据库并键入：

 CREATE EXTENSION postgis;

如果您想要不同的功能（包括我们对地理编码器所需的功能），您需要添加其他扩展（也打包在 Postgres 应用程序中），但我们下次可以专注于此。

现在，如果您有一个 .shp 或 .gdb 文件并想将其放入您的数据库中，您可以使用ogr2ogr轻松做到这一点，它再次随 Postgres App 一起提供。 您可能需要调整一些参数，但基本模式是：

 ogr2ogr Pg:'dbname=DBNAME host=HOST user=USER port=5432' YOUR_FILE.shp

接下来，我将尝试深入研究如何将其转换为地理编码器。



原文:

If you've worked with data before, you've probably heard of Postgres, but if you haven't, Postgres is a leading open source relational database started over 30 years ago. Amazon Redshift was famously built on it and now Google just released AlloyDB built on it. On version 14 with the open source version, it's incredibly easy to get projects set up on your Mac by downloading the Postgres App. You don't have to get the app and you can download only what you want, but if you want a quick way to get started with Postgis for geospatial analysis, go ahead and get the app, which comes with the files necessary for postgis.

What's PostGIS? It's is a spatial extension to Postgres, which allows you to store data types like point and polygon rather than trying to store coordinates as decimals. You can then do geospatial analysis right from your database. For example, if you have a geographical area and you want to find the center, you can run a query with ST_Centroid() with an individual geometry or an entire column.

You can even develop a geocoder in your database. I'll dive into that in my follow up on this post.

So, once you have the Postgres App installed, how do you set up Postgis? First, create a new database (do not do this in the Postgres database).

CREATE DATABASE geocoder;

After you've granted all access and privileges to your user, all you need to do to make it into a Postgis-enabled database is to go into the database and type:

CREATE EXTENSION postgis;

If you want to different capabilities (including what we'll need for the geocoder), you'll need to add other extensions (also packaged in the Postgres App), but we can focus on that next time.

Now, if you have a .shp or .gdb file and want to put it in your database, you can easily do that with ogr2ogr, which again, ships with the Postgres App. ogr2ogr will create a table in your database that mirrors the attributes of the file you're loading. You may have to adjust some of the parameters, but the basic pattern is:

ogr2ogr Pg:'dbname=DBNAME host=HOST user=USER port=5432' YOUR_FILE.shp

Next, I'll try to do a dive into how to convert this into a geocoder.
item.author: cincybc

item.title: 我最喜欢的 3 个 CSS 技巧 | CSS isn't magic. My 3 favorite CSS tricks
item.description:
很多时候，使用边距和填充速记的做法会导致将来代码维护的复杂性。 问题是人们在不需要时设置了 0 值。 例如，如果他们必须将顶部和底部填充设置为 1rem，他们将编写padding: 1rem 0 ;

在这种情况下，0 值将导致您将来必须覆盖它。 并且每次都这样做。 所以你会去超越地狱。

作为一种解决方案，我建议使用新的逻辑margin-block 、 margin-inline 、 padding-block和padding-inline属性。

不要这样做

.example { padding : 1rem 0 ; } /* or */ .example { padding : 0 1rem ; }

你可以改用这个

.example { padding-block : 1rem ; } /* or */ .example { padding-inline : 1rem ; }

每当我看到具有固定高度的 textarea 时，我都想尖叫：“给我用户友好的 textarea”。 我想舒适地输入数据。 给我这个。

我理解开发人员这样做是因为 textarea 更改会破坏布局。 但我们可以找到更优雅的解决方案。

设置一个最小的舒适高度并保存它的大小，但使用resize: vertical禁用宽度更改。 而且您的用户不会偶然破坏布局。

不要这样做

textarea { height : 10rem ; resize : none ; }

你可以改用这个

textarea { min-height : 10rem ; max-height : 15rem ; resize : vertical ; }

当我们的网站变得流行时，翻译不同语言的重要问题。 例如，我经常想将网站从英语翻译成阿拉伯语。

问题如下，英语是行首在左侧（LTR）而在阿拉伯语中行首在右侧（RTL）的语言问题。

因此，如果我为阿拉伯语用户使用text-align: left ，他们会感到困惑，因为行的开头会像他想的那样在左边而不是右边。

这是因为左右值不考虑文本方向，即当我们使用左值或右值时，文本总是与左边缘或右边缘对齐。

但是我们可以使用考虑文本方向的start和end值来修复它。 如果我们用户的浏览器设置为 LTR 语言，则该行的开头将在左侧。 如果它是用 RTL 语言设置的，那是正确的。

不要这样做

.example { text-align : left ; } /* or */ .example { text-align : right ; }

你可以改用这个

.example { text-align : start ; } /* or */ .example { text-align : end ; }

我的其他文章

附言
请使用我的电子邮件进行交流👉 melnik909@ya.ru

💪 Discover more free things from me 💪 从我这里发现更多免费的东西



原文:

A lot of time there is the practice of using the margin and padding shorthand that leads to complication of code maintaining in the future. The problem is people set 0 value when it doesn't need. For example, if they have to set top and bottom paddings to 1rem they'll write padding: 1rem 0;

In this case, the 0 value will lead to you have to override it in the future. And do that every time. So you will go to overrides hell.

As a solution, I recommend using new logical margin-block, margin-inline, padding-block and padding-inline properties.

don't do this

.example { padding: 1rem 0;
} /* or */ .example { padding: 0 1rem;
}

you can use this instead

.example { padding-block: 1rem;
} /* or */ .example { padding-inline: 1rem;
}

resize: none vs resize: vertical

Whenever I see textarea with a fixed height I want to scream: "Give me userfriendly textarea". I want to enter data comfortably. Give me this.

I understand developers do that because textarea changing breaks the layout. But we can find a more elegant solution.

Set a minimal comfortable height and save resizing of it but disable width changes using resize: vertical. And your users will not break the layout by chance.

don't do this

textarea { height: 10rem; resize: none;
}

you can use this instead

textarea { min-height: 10rem; max-height: 15rem; resize: vertical;
}

The start and end keywords

When our website becomes popular there is the important issue of translating on different languages. For example, I often wanted to translate the website from English to Arabic.

The problem is following, English is a matter of languages where the beginning of the line is on the left (LTR) and in Arabic the beginning of the line is on the right (RTL).

So if I use the text-align: left for Arabic users they will be confused because the beginning of the line will be by the left and no right like he thought.

It happens because the left and right values don't consider text direction, i.e when we use the left or right value a text is aligned to the left or right edge always.

But we can fix it using the start and end values that consider the text direction. If a browser of our user is setting in LTR language the beginning of the line will be by left. And if it's setting in RTL language then by right.

don't do this

.example { text-align: left;
} /* or */ .example { text-align: right;
}

you can use this instead

.example { text-align: start;
} /* or */ .example { text-align: end;
}

My others articles
